just don't make big decisions from the bottom.

Bad days don't move in for good.
They're just crashing on your couch for a night or two.
Once they leave,
you'll notice you actually got through it,
and you're not as broken as you thought.
en.editorNote: For someone in the middle of a really rough patch. You don't have to feel better right now — just survive till morning. Making it through today is the win.
variations: Bad days aren't moving in. They're just crashing on your couch for a night.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by manager today motivational essays about trusting the long arc of effort

zh-tw.content:
你現在做的事，
還沒長出果實，
所以看起來像在浪費時間。

但種子在土裡的時候，
誰也看不到它在動。

你不是沒進步，
你只是還在地底下。
等該破土的那天到了，
旁邊的人才會驚訝——
你居然已經走這麼遠了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給那種努力很久卻看不到回報、開始懷疑自己的人。看不見的東西不代表沒在發生，給自己一點時間。
variations: 種子在土裡看起來什麼都沒做，其實它正在最忙的時候。

en.content:
What you're doing right now
hasn't grown fruit yet,
so it looks like wasted time.

But a seed underground —
nobody can see it moving either.

You're not failing to grow.
You're still under the soil.
The day you finally break through,
the people around you will be the surprised ones —
"how did you get this far already?"
en.editorNote: For anyone grinding with no visible payoff, starting to doubt themselves. Invisible doesn't mean nothing's happening. Give it time.
variations: Underground a seed looks lazy. That's actually when it's working hardest.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by self-doubt management essays about action preceding confidence

zh-tw.content:
你不是要等有信心才去做，
是去做了才會有信心。

信心不是天生長在身上的，
它是你做完一件你以為自己做不到的事之後，
才慢慢長出來的東西。

所以下次你又懷疑自己的時候，
別等心情好，
先動手，
證據會自己累積。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給每天都在等「準備好」、等「有自信」才願意開始的人。信心不是起點，是副產品，先動就對了。
variations: 信心是你做完之後才出現的東西，不是做之前的條件。

en.content:
You're not waiting for confidence to act —
you act, and confidence shows up later.

Confidence isn't something you're born with.
It's what slowly grows
after you do a thing you didn't think you could.

So next time you start doubting yourself,
don't wait to feel ready.
Move first.
The proof piles up on its own.
en.editorNote: For anyone stuck waiting to "feel ready" or "feel confident" before starting. Confidence is the receipt, not the entry fee. Move first.
variations: Confidence is the receipt you get after doing the thing, not the ticket to begin.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Chinese-language essays on solitude during quiet effort phases

zh-tw.content:
你現在做的事，
身邊的人可能看不懂，
甚至覺得你在浪費時間。

沒關係，
他們不是在你的時區裡。

你不用每件事都解釋給每個人聽，
你只要繼續走，
等結果出來那天，
看不懂的人會自己安靜。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給那種正在做一件還沒辦法跟人解釋的事的人。不被理解很正常，你不欠任何人翻譯。
variations: 你不用每件事都翻譯給別人聽，他們不在你的時區裡。

en.content:
What you're working on right now —
the people around you might not get it.
Some might even think you're wasting time.

That's fine.
They're not in your timezone.

You don't owe everyone an explanation
for what you're building.
Just keep going.
When the results land,
the ones who didn't get it will go quiet on their own.
en.editorNote: For anyone working on something that's hard to explain to friends or family. Being misunderstood is normal — you don't owe anyone a translation.
variations: You don't owe anyone a translation of your life. They're just not in your timezone yet.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by comparison-trap commentary in bilingual quote compilations

zh-tw.content:
你滑手機看別人發光，
以為自己落後。

其實你只是看到他們的精華片段，
沒看到他們刪掉的那五百次。

人生不是同一條跑道，
是各自的山路。
你慢，不代表你輸，
只代表你走的是別人沒走過的那條。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給那種一打開社群就開始焦慮的人。你看到的是別人剪過的版本，自己的原片不用拿去比。
variations: 你看到的是他們的精華，不是他們的原片，別拿來跟自己比。

en.content:
You scroll and see everyone glowing
and assume you're falling behind.

What you don't see
is the five hundred takes they deleted.

Life isn't one shared track.
It's separate mountain paths.
Going slow doesn't mean losing —
it just means you're on the route nobody else has walked.
en.editorNote: For the person whose mood drops every time they open Instagram. You're comparing your raw footage to someone else's highlight reel. Stop doing that.
variations: You're watching their highlight reel and judging it against your raw footage. Of course it doesn't match.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by personal growth reflections about discomfort being the price of change

zh-tw.content:
如果你最近覺得有點不舒服，
做什麼都卡卡的，
別急著診斷自己出問題。

那種彆扭的感覺，
通常是你在長大。

舊的衣服穿不下了，
新的還沒上身，
中間那段最尷尬，
但也最值得撐過去。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給那種「最近怪怪的、沒生病但就是不對勁」的人。那不是壞事，那是你在脫殼，再撐一下下就過去了。
variations: 彆扭的感覺通常不是出問題，是你在長大，舊的版本撐不住你了。

en.content:
If lately you've been feeling off,
everything kind of friction-y,
don't rush to diagnose yourself as broken.

That awkward feeling
is usually you outgrowing something.

The old version doesn't fit anymore,
the new one isn't fully here yet,
and that in-between part is the most uncomfortable —
and the most worth getting through.
en.editorNote: For anyone who feels weirdly off lately even though nothing's technically wrong. That's not a malfunction — that's you molting. Hang in there.
variations: Feeling off usually isn't a malfunction. It's you outgrowing the version of yourself you used to be.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by small-wins and dopamine-momentum themed compilations

zh-tw.content:
今天只完成了一件小事，
你可能覺得不夠。

但一年後回頭看，
你會發現你不是靠某一天大爆發才走到這裡的，
是靠那三百多個「不夠」的日子拼起來的。

小事不小，
它只是還沒變大而已。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給那種覺得自己每天進度太少、根本看不到改變的人。改變不是某天突然發生，是你早就在累積，只是還沒到看得見的時候。
variations: 你今天做的小事，是未來那個你回頭感謝的轉捩點。

en.content:
You only got one small thing done today,
and it doesn't feel like enough.

But a year from now,
you'll look back and realize
you didn't get here from one big breakthrough day —
you got here from three hundred "not enough" ones stitched together.

Small things aren't small.
They just haven't grown up yet.
en.editorNote: For anyone feeling like their daily progress is too tiny to matter. Change doesn't drop in one big moment — it's already happening, you just can't see it yet.
variations: The tiny thing you did today is what future-you will quietly point to as the turning point.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by perseverance and never-giving-up Chinese motivational compilations

zh-tw.content:
你想放棄的那一刻，
通常不是因為你做不到，
是因為你已經做很久了。

累不代表錯，
累代表你在認真做事。

再撐一下，
不是要你硬撐到斷掉，
是因為很多人就是差那一口氣，
才提早離開了那個本來會發生的好結果。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給那種已經努力很久、最近開始想算了的人。你不是不行，你只是太久沒被肯定。再走一段，不要在這裡停。
variations: 想放棄通常不是能力問題，是你撐太久沒人拍肩膀說做得好。

en.content:
The moment you want to quit
usually isn't because you can't do it.
It's because you've already been doing it for a long time.

Being tired doesn't mean you're wrong.
It means you're actually doing the work.

Hang on a little longer —
not until you snap,
but because so many people leave
just before the good thing
would have shown up.
en.editorNote: For anyone who's been at it forever and is starting to think about giving up. You're not failing. You're just under-appreciated. Don't quit in the dark.
variations: Wanting to quit usually isn't about ability. It's about going too long without anyone telling you you're doing fine.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 encouragement compilations on rewriting internal narratives

zh-tw.content:
你卡住不是因為事情太難，
是因為你一直跟自己說同一個故事——

「我不是這塊料」、
「我運氣就是差」、
「我做什麼都沒用」。

那個故事是你寫的，
所以你也可以改。

換一句話對自己說，
不用一夕變正向，
從「也許這次會不一樣」開始就好。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給那種腦子裡有自動播放負面台詞的人。那些話不是事實，是劇本，劇本可以改。
variations: 你不是真的不行，是你跟自己講的故事太老了，該換一版。

en.content:
You're not stuck because it's too hard.
You're stuck because you keep telling yourself the same story —

"I'm not built for this,"
"my luck is just bad,"
"nothing I do works out."

You wrote that story,
which means you can rewrite it.

Say one different thing to yourself.
You don't have to flip to full positivity overnight.
"Maybe this time will be different" is enough to start.
en.editorNote: For anyone with an inner monologue stuck on repeat. Those lines aren't facts — they're a script. Scripts get rewritten all the time.
variations: You're not actually broken. The story you've been telling yourself is just outdated and needs a new draft.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 quote roundups about quiet courage and showing up

zh-tw.content:
勇敢不一定是站上舞台、
大聲講出口、
或做出什麼了不起的決定。

勇敢有時候是
睡不好的隔天還是準時起床、
被拒絕之後還是按了下一封信的寄出、
哭完臉沒洗乾淨就繼續打開那份檔案。

你今天有出現，
就已經比一半的人勇敢了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給那種覺得自己什麼大事都沒做、不算勇敢的人。出現本身就是勇氣，安靜的那種特別值錢。
variations: 你今天有起床、有出現、有再試一次，那已經是勇敢了。

en.content:
Brave doesn't always mean standing on a stage,
saying it out loud,
or making some huge decision.

Sometimes brave is
getting up on time after a sleepless night,
hitting send on the next pitch after being rejected,
opening the file again with your face still puffy from crying.

If you showed up today,
you're already braver than half the room.
en.editorNote: For anyone who thinks they don't count as brave because they didn't do anything dramatic. Showing up is the courage. The quiet kind is the most valuable.
variations: If you got up, showed up, and tried again today — that already counts as brave.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by self-encouragement essays on choosing future-self over comfort-self

zh-tw.content:
你現在做的每個小決定，
其實都是寫信給未來的你。

今天多走那十分鐘、
多寫那一段、
多忍住那一句、
多睡那六小時——

半年後那個你，
會打開信，
然後跟你說一聲謝謝。

不要對未來的自己太壞，
他什麼都記得。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給那種正在猶豫要不要偷懶、要不要走捷徑的人。你不是在跟今天的自己拔河，你是在替半年後的自己選一個處境。
variations: 你今天的每個選擇，半年後都會收到回信。寄好一點。

en.content:
Every small choice you make right now
is basically a letter you're mailing to future-you.

The extra ten minutes you walked today,
the paragraph you didn't skip,
the sentence you held back,
the six hours you actually slept —

future-you, half a year from now,
will open the letter
and quietly say thanks.

Don't be cruel to future-you.
They remember everything.
en.editorNote: For anyone tempted to take the lazy route or the shortcut today. You're not fighting your current self — you're picking the situation your future self has to live in.
variations: Every choice today is a letter to future-you. Try to send the kind they'll be glad to open.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 comparison-focused quote roundups on personal pace

zh-tw.content:
你不是落後，
你只是走在一條
別人不知道的路上。

別人的時間表是別人的，
你的時間表不需要被檢查。

你走得慢，
不代表你走錯，
只是代表你在認真看路。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給那種一打開社群就覺得自己沒救的人。你不是比較晚，你只是沒在跑同一場比賽。
variations: 別人的進度條不是你的，關掉就好。

en.content:
You're not behind.
You're just on a path
nobody else can see.

Their timeline belongs to them.
Yours doesn't need to be inspected.

Moving slowly
doesn't mean you're moving wrong.
It means you're actually paying attention.
en.editorNote: For anyone who feels doomed after five minutes of scrolling. You're not late. You're just not in the same race they're running.
variations: Their progress bar isn't yours. You can close the tab.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Mary Anne Radmacher style quiet-courage resilience quotes

zh-tw.content:
韌性不是把自己撐成
打不倒的鋼鐵。

韌性是今天哭完，
明天還是把鬧鐘設好；

是被打趴了之後，
小聲跟自己說一句：
「明天再試一次。」

那句小聲的話，
才是真正在救你的東西。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給那種覺得自己不夠堅強的人。堅強不是不哭，是哭完還願意再來一次。那已經很厲害了。
variations: 真正的堅強不是不會倒，是倒了還願意小聲說一句明天再來。

en.content:
Resilience isn't turning yourself
into something that can't be hurt.

Resilience is crying tonight
and still setting the alarm for tomorrow.

It's getting knocked flat
and quietly telling yourself,
"I'll try again tomorrow."

That quiet sentence
is the thing actually keeping you alive.
en.editorNote: For anyone who thinks they're not tough enough. Tough isn't never falling apart — it's falling apart and still showing up tomorrow.
variations: Strong isn't never breaking. Strong is breaking and still whispering 'I'll try again tomorrow.'

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 encouragement quote compilations on showing yourself kindness

zh-tw.content:
你會幫朋友找台階下、
替朋友找理由、
跟朋友說「沒事啦這不是你的錯」。

那為什麼
換成是你自己，
你就突然變成全世界
最嚴的那個審判官？

試試看用對朋友的口氣
跟自己講一次話，
你會發現你其實沒那麼糟。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給那種對全世界都很溫柔、只對自己很兇的人。換個語氣對自己說話，很多事不會那麼難過。
variations: 你對朋友那麼好，也分一點給自己吧。

en.content:
You'll find an out for your friend,
make excuses for your friend,
tell your friend "hey, that wasn't your fault."

So why is it
that when it's about you,
you suddenly become
the harshest judge in the building?

Try talking to yourself
the way you'd talk to a friend.
You'll notice you're not actually that bad.
en.editorNote: For anyone who's gentle with everyone except themselves. Switch your inner tone to the one you use with friends — a lot of things stop hurting so much.
variations: You're so kind to your friends. Save a little of that for yourself.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 positive-quote compilations on small wins and momentum

zh-tw.content:
你不一定要今天
搞定整個人生。

洗個碗、回個信、
走出門吹十分鐘風、
把那杯水喝完——

這些都不是廢事，
是今天你贏過昨天的證據。

小贏堆起來，
哪天回頭看，
就是你撐過那段時間的全部理由。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給那種覺得今天什麼都沒做、好沒用的人。小事不是小事，是讓你還站著的那層底。
variations: 今天有把自己照顧好一點點，那就是今天的勝利。

en.content:
You don't have to fix your whole life today.

Wash one dish,
reply to one message,
step outside for ten minutes of air,
finish that glass of water —

none of these are nothing.
They're proof you beat yesterday today.

Small wins stack up,
and one day you'll look back
and realize they're the whole reason you made it through.
en.editorNote: For anyone who thinks they wasted today because nothing big happened. Small stuff isn't small — it's the floor you're still standing on.
variations: If you took slightly better care of yourself today, that already counts as winning.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 uplifting compilations on getting through hard seasons one day at a time

zh-tw.content:
撐過去這件事，
從來不是一口氣吞下整座山。

是今天先撐到中午，
中午撐到下班，
下班撐到洗完澡，
洗完澡撐到躺平。

你不是非得一次處理完所有事，
你只要撐到下一個小段落，
那一段你就贏了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給正在過很難一段時間的人。不用想太遠，先撐到今晚刷完牙就好，明天的事明天再說。
variations: 難關不是一次跨過去的，是一小段一小段熬過去的。

en.content:
Getting through it
was never about swallowing the whole mountain at once.

It's making it to lunch.
Lunch to clock-out.
Clock-out to a hot shower.
Shower to lying flat in bed.

You don't have to solve everything in one go.
You just have to make it to the next small checkpoint —
and that round, you've already won.
en.editorNote: For anyone deep inside a hard stretch. Don't look too far ahead. Just make it to brushing your teeth tonight. Tomorrow's stuff is tomorrow's problem.
variations: Hard times aren't crossed in one leap. They're survived one little stretch at a time.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 resilience quote roundups on the difference between healing and pretending

zh-tw.content:
你不用裝沒事，
才算走出來。

真正的療癒
不是把那段事擦掉，
是有一天你想起它的時候，
還是會難過，
但你已經有力氣
把自己抱回來。

不假裝，
才走得比較遠。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給那種以為「不哭了就是好了」的人。療癒不是消失，是你跟那段事可以同時存在，而你還站著。
variations: 好起來不是忘記，是想起來不再把自己撞碎。

en.content:
You don't have to fake being fine
to count as healed.

Real healing
isn't erasing the thing that hurt.
It's noticing one day
that remembering it still aches —
but now you have the strength
to pull yourself back in.

You go further
when you stop pretending.
en.editorNote: For anyone who thinks healed means never feeling it again. Healing isn't deletion — it's you and the memory coexisting, and you still standing.
variations: Healed doesn't mean forgotten. It means remembering without falling apart.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Ariel Hsu 2026 positive-energy roundup on failure being a restart, not an ending

zh-tw.content:
失敗不是結局，
是一個讓你重新挑位子坐的機會。

你被打掉重練的時候，
不是退回原點，
是帶著上一次的經驗
回到比較好的起跑線。

所以這一次跌倒，
也只是讓下一次站起來
站得比較穩而已。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給那種覺得「我又搞砸了，完了」的人。沒完，你只是拿到了下一次更好的開場。
variations: 跌倒的價值，是讓你下次知道怎麼站得比較穩。

en.content:
Failure isn't the ending.
It's the chance to pick a better seat.

When you get knocked back to the start,
you're not actually at zero —
you're back at the line
with everything the last round taught you.

This fall
is just the reason
the next time you stand up,
you'll stand steadier.
en.editorNote: For anyone in the "I blew it, I'm done" spiral. You're not done. You just unlocked a better version of the next attempt.
variations: Falling is worth it if next time you know how to land on both feet.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 daily positive-quote compilations on showing up even when motivation is gone

zh-tw.content:
有動力的時候做事，
那叫順其自然。

沒動力的時候
還願意伸手做一點點，
那才叫真的厲害。

今天你不想動、
什麼都提不起勁，
但你還是
坐到了那張椅子上、
打開了那個檔案——

那一刻，
你已經贏過很多人，
也贏過昨天的自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給那種覺得「我今天根本沒在狀態」的人。狀態爛還願意出現，本身就是一種厲害。
variations: 有動力做事普通，沒動力還做才是練功。

en.content:
Working when you feel like it
is just going with the flow.

Doing a tiny bit
when you absolutely don't feel like it —
that's the impressive part.

Today you didn't want to move.
Nothing felt worth starting.
But you still
sat down in that chair,
opened that file —

and right there,
you already beat a lot of people,
including yesterday's version of you.
en.editorNote: For anyone telling themselves "I'm just not on today." Showing up off your A-game is its own kind of strong.
variations: Working when motivated is normal. Working without it is the actual flex.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 Gen Z motivational quote roundup on scrolling, comparison, and borrowed confidence

zh-tw.content:
你滑到的那些光鮮亮麗，
是別人挑了三十張才放上去的那一張。

你看到的是他的成果，
沒看到他刪掉的二十九次失敗、
哭過的那幾個晚上、
還有沒拍進去的卡關時間。

所以
不要拿你的幕後，
去比別人的封面。

你過的是整部電影，
他放的只是預告片。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給滑完IG就覺得自己很失敗的人。別人放的是精選集，不是全集，你看到的本來就只是他們想讓你看到的部分。
variations: 別人放的是精華，你卻拿來跟自己的日常比，這場比較從一開始就不公平。

en.content:
That glossy post you just scrolled past
is the one they picked out of thirty tries.

You saw the result.
You didn't see
the twenty-nine versions they deleted,
the nights they cried,
or the hours they sat stuck.

So stop
comparing your behind-the-scenes
to someone else's cover shot.

You're living the whole movie.
They only posted the trailer.
en.editorNote: For anyone closing Instagram feeling like a failure. You're seeing their highlight reel, not the bloopers — the comparison was rigged before it started.
variations: Their feed is the trailer. Don't judge your full movie against someone else's two-minute cut.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 self-doubt and confidence quote collections on the inner critic voice

zh-tw.content:
你腦子裡那個說
「你不夠好」的聲音，
不是事實，
它只是一個比較大聲的房客。

它住在你腦袋很久了，
所以你以為它就是你，
其實它只是
你以前為了保護自己
養出來的習慣。

你可以聽它說話，
但你不用照它的話做。

它說話，
你選擇。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給那種腦子裡一直有個聲音在唱衰自己的人。那不是你，那只是一個你住太久的房客，你才是房東。
variations: 腦袋裡那個批評你的聲音，不是真相，只是住比較久的習慣。

en.content:
That voice in your head
telling you you're not enough
isn't the truth.
It's just a loud tenant.

It's lived in your head so long
you started thinking it was you.
It's not.
It's a habit
you built a long time ago
to keep yourself safe.

You can hear it talk.
You don't have to do what it says.

It speaks.
You choose.
en.editorNote: For anyone whose inner critic won't shut up. That voice isn't you — it's just a loud roommate. You're still the landlord.
variations: The critical voice in your head isn't truth. It's a tenant that overstayed.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 zero-regrets quote collection on time, small choices, and showing up for your own life

zh-tw.content:
你以為改變人生
要等一個很大的決定，
其實是一連串很小的選擇。

今天多睡十分鐘還是早起散步、
滑手機還是讀那兩頁、
找藉口還是傳出那則訊息——

每一個你以為「沒差啦」的選擇，
都在偷偷把你帶往某個方向。

你不用今天翻轉一切，
你只要
在今天的那幾個小路口
選對一次就好。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給那種在等「人生大轉變時刻」的人。改變從來不是一個大爆炸，是你今天那幾個小路口選了哪邊。
variations: 人生不是被一個大決定改變的，是被一堆「沒差啦」的小選擇悄悄改寫的。

en.content:
You think changing your life
takes one big decision.
It doesn't.
It takes a string of small ones.

Ten more minutes of sleep
or a morning walk.
Scrolling
or reading those two pages.
Making an excuse
or sending the message.

Every choice you brushed off as "it doesn't matter"
is quietly steering you somewhere.

You don't have to flip your whole life today.
Just pick the right turn
at one of today's small intersections.
That's enough.
en.editorNote: For anyone waiting for the big turning-point moment. Change isn't an explosion — it's which way you turned at today's small forks.
variations: Your life isn't changed by one big decision. It's quietly rewritten by a hundred small "whatever" ones.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 analysis-paralysis quote collections on the gap between thinking and doing

zh-tw.content:
想三天
沒有比做一次學到更多。

你在腦子裡演了一百場，
每一場都失敗，
所以你以為這件事很難——

其實你只是
還沒讓現實出來投票。

別再開內心的會議了，
那些焦慮全都是
你自己一個人在跟自己吵。

做一次，
世界會直接告訴你答案，
比你想三天都準。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給那種一件小事可以在腦子裡演一週的人。腦內小劇場永遠是最壞版本，做一次的真實結果通常比你想的溫柔很多。
variations: 想三天不如做一次，你以為是難，其實是還沒給現實一個機會。

en.content:
Three days of thinking
teaches you less than one try.

You ran a hundred scenarios in your head,
and every single one failed,
so you decided this thing is hard.

Really,
you just never let reality cast a vote.

Stop holding the meeting inside your skull.
All that anxiety
is you arguing with yourself in an empty room.

Try it once.
The world will give you an answer
faster and cleaner
than three days of overthinking ever will.
en.editorNote: For anyone who can spin a tiny decision into a week-long mental drama. Your brain always picks the worst-case ending. Reality tends to be way kinder than the rehearsal.
variations: One try teaches more than three days of overthinking. You don't have a problem — you have a vote reality hasn't cast yet.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 self-acceptance and inferiority quote collections on being whole even when you're not perfect

zh-tw.content:
你不用先變成更好的版本，
才有資格喜歡自己。

那種「等我瘦下來」、
「等我升職」、
「等我把脾氣改掉」
再來愛自己的契約，
是世界上最爛的合約。

因為等你做到，
你又會找到下一個還不夠的地方。

現在這個有點亂、
有點沒自信、
有時候還會搞砸的你，
就已經是合格的版本了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給那種一直在等自己變好才肯對自己好的人。那張清單永遠寫不完，現在這個你就已經夠資格被你自己喜歡了。
variations: 你不需要先變完美，才能開始喜歡自己。現在這版本就過關了。

en.content:
You don't have to become a better version of yourself
before you're allowed to like yourself.

That contract that says
"I'll love myself once I lose the weight,
once I get the promotion,
once I fix my temper" —
that's the worst deal on the market.

Because the second you hit it,
you'll find something new that's not enough yet.

The current you —
a little messy,
a little unsure,
the one who still drops the ball sometimes —
that version already qualifies.
en.editorNote: For anyone who keeps stalling self-kindness until they earn it. That list never ends. The current you is already eligible.
variations: You don't have to be perfect first to start liking yourself. The current version already passes.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 overthinking and mental clarity quote roundups on the noise inside your head

zh-tw.content:
你不是真的有那麼多事要煩，
你只是
腦子沒關機而已。

那些半夜冒出來的小劇場、
還沒發生的最壞情況、
別人那句話的十二種解讀——

大多數都不會變成真的，
它們只是你大腦的背景音樂
太大聲了。

試試看深呼吸三下，
把音量轉小，
你會發現眼前其實
沒有想像中那麼多事。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給那種腦子停不下來、什麼都能想成大事的人。九成的煩惱不是真的問題，只是腦子忘了關靜音。
variations: 你沒有那麼多麻煩，只是腦子沒關靜音。

en.content:
You don't actually have that much to worry about.
Your brain just won't power down.

The 3 a.m. mental theater,
the worst-case scenarios that haven't happened,
the twelve possible meanings
of what they said —

most of it never becomes real.
It's just background noise
your head is playing too loud.

Take three deep breaths.
Turn the volume down.
What's actually in front of you
is way smaller than what's in your head.
en.editorNote: For anyone whose brain refuses to shut up. Ninety percent of your problems aren't real problems — they're just your head forgetting the mute button.
variations: You don't have that many problems. Your brain just forgot to hit mute.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 motivational quote roundups on showing up imperfectly and the value of unfinished effort

zh-tw.content:
做得有點爛，
勝過完美地沒做。

你那個還沒寫完的草稿、
還沒寄出去的訊息、
還沒開始的計畫——

它們不是因為太難才卡住，
是因為你想等到
「夠好」才出手。

但「夠好」不會自己來，
它是你出手之後
才慢慢被磨出來的。

先把那個爛版本生出來，
它就已經贏過
你腦子裡那個完美但不存在的版本。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給那種想做到完美才肯動手、結果什麼都沒做的人。爛版本完成日，就是完美版本誕生的開始。
variations: 做得爛總比沒做強，先有東西才談得上修。

en.content:
A messy attempt
beats a perfect non-attempt.

That unfinished draft,
that unsent message,
that plan that's still "about to start" —

they're not stuck because they're hard.
They're stuck because you're waiting
until it's "good enough" to act.

But "good enough" doesn't show up on its own.
It gets shaped
after you start.

Ship the rough version.
It already wins
against the perfect version
that only exists in your head.
en.editorNote: For anyone waiting for perfect before they start, and ending up with nothing. The day the rough version exists is the day the polished version becomes possible.
variations: A bad version that exists beats a perfect one that doesn't. Make the thing first, fix it later.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 self-doubt and action quote roundups, including the theme that hesitation feeds fear while action dissolves it

zh-tw.content:
懷疑會越想越大，
行動會越做越小。

你坐在那邊想
「我會不會做不到」、
「萬一搞砸了怎麼辦」、
「別人會怎麼看我」——

每多想一分鐘，
那件事在你心裡
就又長大一吋。

但只要你開始動，
哪怕只是
打開檔案、
寫第一句話、
按下發送鍵，

你就會發現，
它其實沒有
你想像中那麼大隻。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給那種一直卡在「我準備好了嗎」的人。不是先有信心才行動，是先行動才會長出信心。
variations: 想越久越可怕，做下去就還好。

en.content:
Doubt grows the longer you sit with it.
The task shrinks the moment you start.

You sit there asking,
"What if I can't do it?"
"What if I mess up?"
"What will people think?" —

and every extra minute of thinking
makes the thing
an inch bigger in your head.

But the second you move —
even just
opening the file,
writing the first sentence,
hitting send —

you'll notice
it was never
as huge as you thought.
en.editorNote: For anyone stuck in the "am I ready yet" loop. Confidence doesn't come before action — it grows out of it.
variations: The longer you think, the scarier it gets. The moment you start, it shrinks.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 quote roundups about honoring your own path and the futility of comparing timelines

zh-tw.content:
別人開花的時候，
你還在長根。

看著朋友升職、
同學買房、
社群上一個個過得發光發亮——

會焦慮是正常的，
但別忘了，
每棵樹的時程表
本來就不一樣。

你看不到的，
是別人在土裡
撐了多久；
你以為自己慢的，
其實是在
往下長到別人看不見的地方。

根長得夠深，
花會自己來。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給那種一刷IG就懷疑人生的人。每個人的時間軸不一樣，慢不是輸，是還在打底。
variations: 你不是慢，你只是還在長根。

en.content:
While others are blooming,
you're still growing roots.

Friends getting promoted,
classmates buying homes,
feeds full of people glowing —

the anxiety is normal,
but don't forget:
every tree runs
on its own schedule.

What you can't see
is how long they spent
stuck underground.
What feels slow to you
is actually you growing
where no one can see yet.

Root deep enough,
and the bloom shows up on its own.
en.editorNote: For anyone who spirals after scrolling for ten minutes. Different timelines aren't a competition — slow isn't losing, it's foundation.
variations: You're not behind. You're just still putting down roots.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by resilience quote compilations focused on the small, daily acts of getting back up

zh-tw.content:
你不需要每次都贏，
你只需要
每次都還願意回來。

跌倒不丟臉，
躺著不爬起來才丟臉——
但其實連這個也沒人在意，
因為大家都在忙自己的事。

所以慢慢站起來沒關係，
哭一下再起來也沒關係，
甚至明天再起來也可以。

重點不是你跌幾次，
是你還在這場
屬於你自己的遊戲裡。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給那種覺得自己一直失敗、想放棄的人。能再回來一次，就已經贏過昨天的自己。
variations: 跌倒沒事，能再回來就好。

en.content:
You don't have to win every round.
You just have to
keep showing up.

Falling isn't the embarrassing part.
Staying down would be —
except honestly,
no one's even watching,
because everyone's busy with their own stuff.

So get up slow if you need to.
Cry first, then get up.
Wait until tomorrow if that's what it takes.

The point isn't the falls.
It's that you're still in
your own game.
en.editorNote: For anyone feeling like a serial failure who wants to tap out. Coming back at all is already winning against yesterday.
variations: Falling is fine. Coming back is the whole game.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 encouragement quote collections about working with what you have right now

zh-tw.content:
你不用等到
有更好的設備、
有更多的時間、
有更厲害的人脈，
才能開始那件事。

那些條件
等你開始之後
才會慢慢出現。

現在你手上
那台舊筆電、
那兩個小時的空檔、
那一兩個願意聽你說話的朋友——

就是你的起點。

夠用了，
真的夠用了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給那種一直說「等我準備好」、結果三年過去了還在準備的人。手上有的，就是夠用的。
variations: 你手上現在有的，就是你能開始的全部。

en.content:
You don't have to wait
for better gear,
more free time,
or more impressive contacts
before you start.

Those things
show up after you start,
not before.

The beat-up laptop you have,
the two free hours you've got,
the one or two friends
who'll actually listen —

that's your starting line.

It's enough.
Really, it is.
en.editorNote: For anyone who's been "getting ready" for three years. What you have right now is already enough to start with.
variations: What you've got right now is the whole starter kit.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by mindfulness-flavored encouragement quotes about the inner critic versus your actual self

zh-tw.content:
你對自己講的那些話，
如果是對朋友講的，
你早就被絕交了。

「你怎麼這麼笨」、
「你連這個都做不好」、
「你根本不夠格」——

這些話你一天
對自己講幾次？

試試看明天，
當那個聲音又出現的時候，
你問它：
「如果今天是朋友來找我，
我會這樣回他嗎？」

不會的話，
你也不要這樣對自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給那種對別人很溫柔、對自己很狠的人。你值得用對朋友那種口氣，跟自己說話。
variations: 你對自己的話，留一點給朋友的溫柔。

en.content:
The way you talk to yourself —
if you said any of it to a friend,
they'd block you.

"You're so stupid."
"You can't even do this right."
"You're not good enough."

How many times a day
do you say that to yourself?

Tomorrow, when that voice shows up,
try asking it:
"If a friend came to me with this,
would I really answer like that?"

If the answer is no,
then don't say it to yourself either.
en.editorNote: For anyone who's kind to everyone except themselves. You deserve the same tone you'd use with a friend.
variations: Talk to yourself the way you'd talk to a friend you actually like.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by small-wins themed quote compilations from productivity blogs

zh-tw.content: 今天比昨天多做一點點，就已經贏了昨天的你。
zh-tw.editorNote: 獻給覺得自己進度太小、不值得慶祝的人。一點點累積起來，三個月後你會嚇到自己。
variations: 小小一步也算數，重點是你今天有踏出去。

en.content: Doing a little more than yesterday means you already beat yesterday's you.
en.editorNote: For anyone who thinks small progress isn't worth celebrating. Stack enough small days and three months from now you won't recognize where you started.
variations: Tiny steps still count. The point is that you moved at all.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by resilience and perseverance themes in 2026 motivational quote roundups

zh-tw.content: 難關不會永遠難，會過去的，只是這次比較久一點。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合卡在低潮、覺得這次真的撐不下去的時候。提醒自己過去那些以為熬不過的，後來都熬過了。
variations: 再撐一下下，痛過之後你會發現自己比想像中還硬。

en.content: Hard times don't stay hard forever. They pass. This one's just taking its sweet time.
en.editorNote: For when you're deep in a rough patch and convinced this is the one that breaks you. Every other rough patch you survived thought the same thing.
variations: Hold on a little longer. You're tougher than this season is making you feel.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Hemingway-style quotes about self-comparison versus comparing to others

zh-tw.content: 你不需要贏過誰，你只要贏過昨天那個還沒開始的自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給總愛跟別人比、然後覺得自己很爛的人。你的對手從來不是隔壁那位，是還沒動起來的自己。
variations: 別人的進度條跟你沒關係，把自己的跑完就好。

en.content: You don't need to beat anyone else. You just need to beat the version of you who hadn't started yet.
en.editorNote: For the chronic comparers who feel inadequate every time they open social media. Your only real competition is the you from last week.
variations: Other people's progress bars aren't yours. Just finish your own.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by zero-regrets themed quote essays for 2026

zh-tw.content: 怕失敗很正常，但別讓「沒試過」變成你十年後最後悔的事。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種想做但一直不敢做的人。失敗你還可以重來，沒試過的事會跟你一輩子。
variations: 搞砸了還有故事可以講，沒做的話連故事都沒有。

en.content: Being scared to fail is normal. Just don't let "I never tried" become your biggest regret ten years from now.
en.editorNote: For the person who's been sitting on a dream for years. Failure you can recover from. "What if" follows you around forever.
variations: If you mess up, you get a story. If you don't try, you don't even get that.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by encouragement quotes about doing better than you think you are

zh-tw.content: 說個冷知識：你做得比你以為的還要好，只是你自己沒發現而已。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給那種總覺得自己不夠好的人。從旁邊看你的人，其實都覺得你蠻厲害的，只是沒人跟你說。
variations: 你眼中的「還不夠」，在別人眼中已經是「好猛」。

en.content: Fun fact: you're doing better than you think. You're just too close to see it.
en.editorNote: For everyone stuck in the "I'm not doing enough" loop. The people watching you from outside see way more than you give yourself credit for.
variations: What you call "not enough" looks pretty impressive from where everyone else is standing.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by themes around small wins stacking into bigger results

zh-tw.content: 今天做的那一點點，明年回頭看會很感謝自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給覺得「這點努力哪有什麼用」的人，所有看起來厲害的成果，都是某個沒人看見的日子默默累積出來的。
variations: 小小的進度也是進度，不要小看每天那一點點。

en.content: The tiny thing you did today is what next-year-you will thank you for.
en.editorNote: For anyone tempted to skip because "it's too small to matter." Big results are just stacks of small days nobody clapped for.
variations: Small progress still counts. Don't shrug off the little daily wins.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by healing-themed quote essays about getting through low moments

zh-tw.content: 你不用馬上好起來，你只要今天比昨天好一點點就夠了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合卡在低潮、卻又被「快點振作」這種話壓得喘不過氣的時候。康復本來就不是直線。
variations: 好起來不用很快，慢慢的、有方向就好。

en.content: You don't have to be okay right now. Just a little better than yesterday is enough.
en.editorNote: For when everyone's telling you to "snap out of it" and you can't. Healing isn't a straight line — it's allowed to be slow.
variations: Getting better doesn't have to be fast. Slow and pointing forward is plenty.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 work-themed motivational quotes about consistency over intensity

zh-tw.content: 不用每天爆發，每天有出現就已經贏一半了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種總想「一次拚到底」結果隔天就累爆放棄的人。能持續出現，比偶爾的大爆發更重要。
variations: 穩穩做的人，最後都贏過拚命衝又掉鏈子的人。

en.content: You don't need to go all-out every day. Just showing up already puts you halfway there.
en.editorNote: For the all-or-nothing types who sprint Monday and burn out by Wednesday. Consistent beats heroic, every single time.
variations: Steady people quietly beat the ones who go hard and then disappear.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 quote compilations about courage and trying again

zh-tw.content: 勇氣不是不害怕，是明明很怕還是去做了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給那種覺得「等我不怕了再做」的人。怕是正常的，會做的人也是邊發抖邊做的。
variations: 等你不怕的那天可能不會來，所以就帶著怕一起上吧。

en.content: Courage isn't being unafraid. It's being scared and doing it anyway.
en.editorNote: For anyone waiting to feel ready before they act. The people you admire weren't fearless — they were just scared and moved anyway.
variations: The day you stop feeling scared might never come. Go anyway, scared and all.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 essays on rebuilding self-trust through small, intentional steps

zh-tw.content: 你不是落後，你只是沒在跟自己比。
zh-tw.editorNote: 滑手機看別人都在發光的時候用。重點不是誰跑得快，是你今天有沒有比昨天的自己多走一點點。
variations: 別人的進度是別人的，你的節奏才是你的。

en.content: You're not behind. You're just measuring yourself against the wrong person.
en.editorNote: For the late-night doomscroll when everyone seems to be winning. The only fair comparison is you today vs. you yesterday.
variations: Their timeline isn't your timeline. Run your own race.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 optimism-themed quote roundups on consistency over motivation

zh-tw.content: 動力會跑掉，但習慣不會。所以靠習慣比較穩。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合那種「我今天沒心情」就放掉的人。心情會變、動力會跑，但動作做久了就變成你自己。
variations: 等有動力才做的話，可能整年都不會開始。

en.content: Motivation comes and goes. Habits stick around. Bet on the habit.
en.editorNote: For anyone who only shows up when they "feel like it." Spoiler: you rarely will. The work has to outlast the mood.
variations: If you wait for motivation, you'll be waiting forever. Just start the thing.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 essays on trusting yourself in a noisy, opinion-saturated world

zh-tw.content: 別人的建議聽一聽就好，最後做決定的人是你。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合容易被一堆人意見搞到頭很痛的時候。聽是禮貌，做不做是你的事，最後負責的也是你。
variations: 意見很多，人生只有一個，你的。

en.content: Take the advice, leave the pressure. You're still the one making the call.
en.editorNote: For when everyone's got an opinion about your life and you can't hear yourself think. Listen, sure — but the final vote is yours.
variations: Plenty of opinions out there. Only one of them has to live your life.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Taiwan positive-energy quote compilations about non-linear progress

zh-tw.content: 退一步不一定是後退，有時候是在拉弓。
zh-tw.editorNote: 卡關、暫停、退回原點的時候給自己看。不是每次往後都叫失敗，有時候是在準備下一次出手。
variations: 停下來不等於走錯，可能只是讓自己看清楚下一步要往哪。

en.content: A step back isn't always going backwards. Sometimes you're just drawing the bow.
en.editorNote: For anyone treating every pause like a setback. Rest, regroup, retreat — all part of moving forward, just not the loud part.
variations: Stopping isn't the same as quitting. Sometimes it's how you aim better.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by small-effort consistency themes around daily habits compounding into results

zh-tw.content: 你每天做一點點的事，最後會變成別人追不上的距離。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合送給覺得自己進度慢到懷疑人生的人。一天看不出來，一年回頭看會嚇到自己。
variations: 今天的一點點，是未來的一大段。

en.content: The tiny thing you do every day is the thing nobody can catch up on later.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who thinks daily habits are too small to matter. Boring on Monday, life-changing by next year.
variations: Small today, unreachable tomorrow.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Taiwan motivational quote themes about failure as restart opportunity

zh-tw.content: 跌倒不丟臉，賴在地上才丟臉。
zh-tw.editorNote: 失敗本身不是問題，問題是你願不願意拍一拍站起來。給最近摔得有點重的朋友看一下。
variations: 重點不是怎麼跌的，是怎麼站起來的。

en.content: Falling isn't embarrassing. Staying on the floor is.
en.editorNote: For anyone using one bad day as a reason to give up on the whole week. You're allowed to flop — just don't move in.
variations: Nobody remembers how you fell. They remember if you got up.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Manager Today compilation themes on self-trust and inner voice

zh-tw.content: 別人怎麼看你不重要，重要的是你看自己的時候，眼神還亮不亮。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在意別人評價到喘不過氣的時候。鏡子裡那個人覺得你還可以，其他人說什麼真的還好。
variations: 別人給的標籤你可以撕掉，自己給自己的眼神最難換。

en.content: What other people see doesn't matter half as much as how your own eyes look back at you.
en.editorNote: For when everyone's opinion is louder than yours. The only review that follows you home is the one from the mirror.
variations: Their take expires. Yours has to live with you.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Taiwan workplace motivational themes around starting messy

zh-tw.content: 醜醜的開始，也比完美的計畫先到終點。
zh-tw.editorNote: 完美主義害死人系列。先做出一個爛版本再說，至少你已經在跑了，計畫派還在 Word 裡寫大綱。
variations: 做出來的爛版本，贏過想出來的好點子。

en.content: An ugly start still beats a perfect plan that never leaves the doc.
en.editorNote: For the perfectionist who keeps polishing the outline instead of shipping the thing. Bad v1 beats brilliant v0.
variations: Done and messy travels faster than perfect and pending.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by compilation themes about comparing yourself to past self instead of others

zh-tw.content: 你要贏的不是別人，是上禮拜那個還在原地打轉的自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 刷 IG 看到別人都好厲害的時候打開來看。賽道不一樣，比也沒用，唯一能比的對象住在你身體裡。
variations: 別人的進度條跟你無關，你只要比昨天多一格就好。

en.content: You're not racing them. You're racing the version of you who was stuck last week.
en.editorNote: Read this before opening Instagram. Everyone's on a different track — the only fair race is you vs. last-week-you.
variations: Their highlight reel isn't your scoreboard.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by themes around courage being action despite fear, not absence of it

zh-tw.content: 勇敢不是不害怕，是手在抖還願意按下去。
zh-tw.editorNote: 面試前、按下送出鍵前、按下分手鍵前都適用。沒有人是不怕的，差別只在你怕著還是做了。
variations: 怕是正常的，怕到不敢動才是問題。

en.content: Brave isn't not scared. Brave is hands shaking and still pressing send.
en.editorNote: For the moment right before the interview, the awkward text, the big ask. Everyone's scared — you just go anyway.
variations: Courage is just fear that decided to move.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Suzy Kassem theme that doubt kills more dreams than failure ever does

zh-tw.content: 讓夢想死掉的通常不是失敗，是你那句「我應該不行吧」。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給那個還沒開始就先把自己刷掉的人。失敗至少代表你出手過，懷疑連門票都沒買。
variations: 你以為的小心，其實只是還沒被打開的可能。

en.content: Most dreams don't die from failure. They die from "I probably can't."
en.editorNote: For the friend who rules themselves out before anyone else gets a chance to. Failing means you tried — doubt means you didn't even buy the ticket.
variations: You can't lose a game you never played, but you can't win one either.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Taiwan compilation themes about small daily efforts accumulating into something big

zh-tw.content: 每天小小做一點，半年後回頭看會嚇到自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 進度慢到懷疑人生的時候打開來看。複利不是只用在錢上，努力也是會利滾利的。
variations: 今天的一公分，是明年的一公里。

en.content: A little every day looks like nothing — until six months later, when it looks like everything.
en.editorNote: Read this when progress feels invisible. Compound interest works on effort too, you just don't see the graph in real time.
variations: Boring consistency beats exciting bursts every time.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by themes about reframing setbacks as plot points rather than endings

zh-tw.content: 這不是結局，只是故事比較難看的那一章。
zh-tw.editorNote: 搞砸的時候、被拒絕的時候、好像什麼都不對的時候用。爛章節是書的一部分，但不是書名。
variations: 難看的那段是劇情，不是結尾。

en.content: This isn't the ending. It's just the chapter nobody screenshots.
en.editorNote: For the messy middle — the rejection, the layoff, the breakup. Rough chapters are part of the book, not the title of it.
variations: Plot twist, not plot end.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Jim Rohn theme that motivation starts you but habit keeps you going

zh-tw.content: 動力負責點火，習慣負責讓火不要熄掉。
zh-tw.editorNote: 三分鐘熱度過了之後最需要看這句。等到不想做還是去做了，那才是真的開始。
variations: 靠心情會斷掉，靠習慣才走得遠。

en.content: Motivation lights the match. Habit keeps the fire from going out.
en.editorNote: Read this on day three, when the hype has worn off. Doing it when you don't feel like it is where the real progress lives.
variations: Feelings start things. Habits finish them.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by themes about small daily disciplines stacking into outsized results

zh-tw.content: 今天那件你覺得「做了也沒差」的小事，半年後會變成別人眼中的天賦。
zh-tw.editorNote: 進度肉眼看不到的時候打開來看。所謂天賦，其實是別人沒看到你做了多少次的累積。
variations: 你嫌小的那一步，剛好就是別人沒走的那一步。

en.content: The thing you think is "too small to matter" today is what people will call your talent in six months.
en.editorNote: For when the work feels invisible. What looks like talent from the outside is just reps nobody saw you put in.
variations: Tiny, boring, repeated — that's the whole recipe.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 themes that growth and comfort cannot coexist

zh-tw.content: 你想要的東西，幾乎都住在你不想做的事情後面。
zh-tw.editorNote: 想瘦、想升職、想會某個技能，答案通常都不舒服。看到這句就知道，那個不想做的，就是該做的。
variations: 舒服跟成長，沒辦法同時挑。

en.content: Almost everything you want is hiding behind something you don't want to do.
en.editorNote: The workout, the awkward email, the hard conversation — that's usually the door. If it feels like resistance, it's probably the way through.
variations: Comfortable and growing — pick one.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by resilience-as-skill themes from Hello Driven's resilience quotes compilation

zh-tw.content:
韌性不是天生的個性，
是一次又一次被打回原形，
還願意重新站起來——
練出來的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 覺得自己不夠堅強的時候看，韌性不是某些人天生有的特權，是每次跌倒後選擇起身的習慣慢慢養成的。
variations: 你以為韌性是被生下來就有的，其實是被生活磨出來的。

en.content:
Resilience isn't a personality trait you're born with.
It's the muscle you build
every time you get knocked down
and decide to get back up anyway.
en.editorNote: For when you feel like you're not strong enough — resilience isn't a gift some people get; it's a habit built one comeback at a time.
variations: You weren't born resilient. You got that way the hard way — and that's exactly why it counts.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by seeds-not-harvest progress themes from Parade inspirational quotes collection

zh-tw.content:
不要用今天收穫了什麼來評斷今天，
用你今天種下了什麼。

有些種子是要等很久才會發芽的，
但你沒種，就什麼都不會長。
zh-tw.editorNote: 在努力很久卻看不到成果時最需要這句話，提醒自己看不見的種子也是真的存在，不要因為還沒開花就否定今天的播種。
variations: 今天的成果是過去的種子，今天的種子是未來的成果。

en.content:
Don't judge today by what you harvested.
Judge it by what you planted.

Some seeds take a long time to sprout —
but if you never plant them, nothing grows.
en.editorNote: For the long stretches where effort feels invisible. Today's planting becomes tomorrow's harvest, even when you can't see it yet.
variations: Today's results are yesterday's seeds. Today's seeds are tomorrow's results.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by start-where-you-are pragmatic action themes from Shutterfly encouraging quotes

zh-tw.content:
從你現在的位置開始，
用你現在手上有的，
做你現在能做的。

不要等更好的時機、更多的資源、更完整的自己，
那些東西，很可能永遠不會剛好同時出現。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那個一直在等「條件齊全」才肯動的人，提醒他完整的條件是奢求，能用的條件才是現實。
variations: 你不需要全部，你只需要夠用的那一部分。

en.content:
Start where you are.
Use what you have.
Do what you can.

Don't wait for a better moment, more resources, or a more complete version of yourself —
those things probably won't all arrive at the same time.
en.editorNote: For the person waiting until every condition lines up before starting. Perfect conditions are a fantasy. Workable conditions are reality.
variations: You don't need everything. You just need enough to begin.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by hidden-reserves and discovering-strength-in-difficulty themes from GMU well-being resilience quotes

zh-tw.content:
你身上其實一直都有那些力量，
只是要等到很難的時候，
才會發現原來自己有。

難關不是來打倒你的，
是來告訴你「你比你以為的還多」。
zh-tw.editorNote: 在挑戰當中或剛走過低谷的人最有共鳴，那些你不知道自己有的東西，往往是在最難的時候才被翻出來的。
variations: 你撐過的每一關，都在偷偷把你的底牌補強。

en.content:
The strength was always in you.
It just takes something hard
to realize you had it the whole time.

Hard moments aren't here to break you —
they're here to show you you're more than you thought.
en.editorNote: Resonates most in the middle of a challenge or just after surviving one. The things you didn't know you had usually surface only when you really need them.
variations: Every hard thing you've gotten through has quietly added to what you can handle next.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by trust-your-intuition and self-trust-as-skill themes from Postgrad Playbook intuition quotes

zh-tw.content:
你心裡那個小小的聲音，
常常比你想得還準。

不是每件事都要問別人，
有時候你自己已經知道答案了，
只是還沒承認而已。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那個習慣什麼決定都要問三個朋友的人，提醒他自己的直覺也是可以信的，不一定要等別人來蓋章。
variations: 你不是不知道答案，你只是在等一個人替你說出來。

en.content:
That small voice inside you
is usually more right than you give it credit for.

Not every decision needs a second opinion.
Sometimes you already know the answer —
you just haven't admitted it yet.
en.editorNote: For the person who polls three friends before every decision — your gut counts as a vote too. You don't always need outside validation to act.
variations: You're not unsure of the answer. You're waiting for someone else to say it out loud first.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by self-trust-is-built-not-found themes from Self Trust Coach quote collection

zh-tw.content:
相信自己不是一種感覺，
是你每次說了會做，就真的做了，
累積出來的東西。

你說的每個小承諾，
做到了，就是在幫自己存錢。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種「我怎麼都不相信自己」的人，提醒自信是行動的結果，不是心情，從履行小承諾開始就有效。
variations: 對自己說了會做的事，做到，就是在重新認識自己的開始。

en.content:
Self-trust isn't a feeling you wake up with.
It's what you build every time you say you'll do something
and then actually do it.

Every small promise you keep to yourself
is a deposit in the account.
en.editorNote: For anyone who thinks self-trust is a personality trait — it's actually a track record. Start with the smallest promises you can keep.
variations: Doing what you said you'd do, even when no one's watching — that's where confidence actually comes from.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by inner peace and self-acceptance themes from Ariel Hsu positive energy compilation

zh-tw.content:
跟自己好好相處，
是一輩子的功課。

不用每天都喜歡自己，
但可以選擇不對自己那麼兇。

那個對你最嚴格的人，
常常不是別人，是你。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那個常常自我責備、覺得自己不夠好的人，不需要瞬間自愛，先從不那麼嚴厲開始就行了。
variations: 你不需要愛上自己的每個部分，但你可以選擇不再罵自己。

en.content:
Learning to be okay with yourself
is a lifelong project.

You don't have to like yourself every day —
but you can choose not to be so hard on yourself.

The harshest critic in your life
isn't usually someone else. It's you.
en.editorNote: For the inner critic who's always running. You don't need to leap straight to self-love — just stop the constant self-attack. That alone changes things.
variations: You don't have to love every part of yourself. You can start by just not yelling at the parts you don't.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by confidence-through-decisions and owning-your-choices themes from Success magazine trust your gut quotes

zh-tw.content:
不用每個選擇都完美。

你做了選擇，然後負責——
這比一直猶豫不選，已經厲害太多。

後悔的選擇可以修正，
沒做的選擇連修的機會都沒有。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那個怕選錯所以乾脆不選的人，提醒他不做選擇也是一種選擇，而且通常代價更大。
variations: 錯的決定還能改，沒做的決定只會繼續卡在原地。

en.content:
Your choices don't all have to be the right ones.

Making a call and owning it
is already miles ahead of staying stuck deciding.

A wrong choice can be corrected.
A choice you never made can't.
en.editorNote: For the person who avoids deciding because they might pick wrong — not choosing is still a choice, and usually a more expensive one.
variations: Bad decisions can be fixed. Decisions you never made just keep you stuck.

items.sourceNote: Discipline-shows-up-when-motivation-fades theme, rewritten originally

zh-tw.content:
動力會來，也會走，
靠它你撐不久。

真正帶你走到最後的，
是那個沒感覺也照樣做的習慣。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那個老是說「等我比較有動力再開始」的人——習慣比熱情可靠太多，三分鐘熱度退了之後，撐住你的從來不是心情。
variations: 熱情負責點火，習慣負責讓火不熄。
你不是缺動力，你是缺一個就算沒動力也會去做的習慣。

en.content:
Motivation comes and goes.
You can't lean on it for long.

What actually carries you to the end
is the habit you keep even when you don't feel like it.
en.editorNote: For the person who keeps saying "I'll start when I feel more motivated." Habits outlast hype every time — once the buzz fades, it's routine, not mood, that keeps you going.
variations: Passion lights the fire. Habit is what keeps it from going out.
You're not short on motivation. You're short on a habit that runs without it.

items.sourceNote: Be-as-kind-to-yourself-as-to-a-friend theme, rewritten originally

zh-tw.content:
你對朋友那麼溫柔，
為什麼對自己那麼兇？

下次想罵自己的時候，
先問：這句話我捨得對好朋友說嗎？
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那個對別人很包容、對自己卻很嚴苛的人。把對朋友的標準拿來對自己，往往就會發現自己一直被自己虧待。
variations: 你願意給朋友的那份體諒，自己也值得一份。
對自己好一點，不是放縱，是你也只是個普通人。

en.content:
You're so gentle with your friends.
Why are you so harsh with yourself?

Next time you're about to tear yourself apart, ask:
would I ever say this to someone I love?
en.editorNote: For the person who extends endless grace to others but none to themselves. Hold yourself to the standard you give your friends and you'll notice how badly you've been treating yourself.
variations: The understanding you hand out so freely? You deserve a share of it too.
Being kind to yourself isn't going soft. It's remembering you're only human too.

items.sourceNote: Fear-of-failure-vs-fear-of-regret theme, rewritten originally

zh-tw.content:
你怕失敗，
但有沒有想過，
你更該怕的是——

十年後回頭，
發現自己當初根本沒試。
zh-tw.editorNote: 在猶豫要不要踏出那一步的時候看。失敗最多痛一陣子，沒試過的後悔會跟你很久，兩個拿來比，答案其實很清楚。
variations: 失敗會過去，沒做過的後悔不會。
與其問「會不會搞砸」，不如問「十年後我會不會後悔沒做」。

en.content:
You're afraid of failing.
But have you considered
the thing you should fear more —

looking back in ten years
and realizing you never even tried?
en.editorNote: For when you're on the fence about taking the leap. Failure stings for a while; the regret of never trying sticks around for years. Put them side by side and the choice gets a lot clearer.
variations: Failure fades. The regret of never trying doesn't.
Instead of "what if I blow it," ask "will I regret not doing it ten years from now?"

items.sourceNote: Bloom-on-your-own-timeline theme, rewritten originally

zh-tw.content:
花不會因為旁邊的先開了，
就覺得自己壞掉了。

它只是還沒到自己的季節。
你也是。
zh-tw.editorNote: 看到同齡人一個個都「到位」了、自己卻好像卡住的時候，這句話很療癒。每個人有自己的花期，晚開不等於不開。
variations: 別人春天開，你可能是秋天開——時間不同，不代表你輸了。
你沒有落後，你只是還在你自己的時區裡。

en.content:
A flower doesn't think it's broken
just because the one beside it bloomed first.

It simply hasn't reached its season yet.
Neither have you.
en.editorNote: For when everyone your age seems to have it figured out and you feel stuck. Everyone blooms on their own clock — late isn't the same as never.
variations: Some bloom in spring, some in fall. A different timing doesn't mean you lost.
You're not behind. You're just on your own timezone.

items.sourceNote: Nothing-in-nature-blooms-all-year self-compassion theme, rewritten originally

zh-tw.content:
對自己也耐心一點。

大自然裡沒有一種東西，
是整年都在盛開的。

你會有低潮、會有空白期，
那不是壞掉，是季節。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那個一沒狀態就開始自責的人，提醒他能量有高有低是自然的事，不是你哪裡出了問題。適合在低潮期傳給自己。
variations: 你不需要一直發光，連太陽都會下山。
休耕的田不是廢田，它只是在等下一個季節。

en.content:
Be patient with yourself, too.

Nothing in nature
blooms all year round.

You'll have low stretches and blank seasons —
that's not broken. That's just a season.
en.editorNote: For the person who starts beating themselves up the moment they're not at their best. Energy rises and falls — that's natural, not a sign something's wrong with you.
variations: You don't have to shine nonstop. Even the sun sets.
A field left fallow isn't a ruined field. It's just waiting for its next season.

items.sourceNote: Patience-is-how-you-wait (not just the waiting) theme, rewritten originally

zh-tw.content:
耐心不是「會不會等」，
是「等的時候，你是什麼樣子」。

同樣在等，
有人焦躁到內傷，
有人安安穩穩過好每一天。

差別不在等多久，在怎麼等。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那個卡在等待期——等結果、等機會、等對的時間——快被自己的焦慮搞瘋的人。提醒他能控制的不是時間，是等待時的狀態。
variations: 等待是每個人都要做的事，怎麼等才是你的選擇。
與其問「還要等多久」，不如問「等的這段時間我想怎麼過」。

en.content:
Patience isn't about whether you can wait.
It's about who you are while you're waiting.

Same wait —
one person stews until it eats them alive,
another just lives each day steadily.

The difference isn't the length. It's the how.
en.editorNote: For anyone stuck in a waiting period — for results, an opening, the right time — and being driven up the wall by their own anxiety. You can't control the clock, but you can control how you wait.
variations: Everyone has to wait. How you wait is the part that's actually yours.
Instead of "how much longer," ask "how do I want to spend the waiting?"

items.sourceNote: Play-your-own-game, keep-the-comparison-internal theme, rewritten originally

zh-tw.content:
要比，就跟昨天的自己比。

別人跑多快不關你的事，
你唯一要回答的問題是：

今天的我，有沒有比昨天好一點點？

哪怕只是一點點，那就是贏。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把「跟別人比」換成「跟自己比」的提醒。前者沒有盡頭也贏不了，後者每天都可能有小小的勝利。適合容易被別人進度影響心情的人。
variations: 唯一值得贏過的對手，是昨天的你。
進步不用很多，只要方向是往前的，今天就算數。

en.content:
If you're going to compare, compare with yesterday's you.

How fast anyone else runs isn't your business.
The only question you have to answer is:

Am I a little better today than I was yesterday?

Even a little. That's a win.
en.editorNote: Swaps comparing-to-others for comparing-to-yourself. The first has no finish line and you can't win it; the second offers a small victory available every single day. Good for anyone whose mood gets hijacked by other people's progress.
variations: The only opponent worth beating is who you were yesterday.
Progress doesn't have to be big. As long as it points forward, today counts.

items.sourceNote: Resilience-is-getting-back-up-not-never-falling theme, rewritten originally

zh-tw.content:
韌性不是從來不跌倒，
是跌了之後，
還願意爬起來再走。

沒有人是不會倒的，
強的人只是比較常選擇站回去。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那個因為又一次失敗而懷疑自己「是不是不夠堅強」的人。堅強不是不會倒，是倒了還願意起來，這件事你已經做過很多次了。
variations: 你不是沒跌倒過，你只是每次都選擇了站起來。
倒下不丟臉，躺著不動才會卡住——你顯然不是後者。

en.content:
Resilience isn't never falling.
It's being willing to get back up
and keep going after you do.

Nobody is unbreakable.
The strong ones just choose to stand back up more often.
en.editorNote: For the person doubting whether they're 'strong enough' after yet another setback. Strength isn't never falling — it's getting up after you do, and you've already done that plenty of times.
variations: It's not that you never fell. It's that you chose to get up every time.
Falling isn't the embarrassing part. Staying down is — and clearly that's not you.

items.sourceNote: There-is-good-in-seeming-failures-time-will-reveal-it theme, rewritten originally

zh-tw.content:
那些現在看起來像失敗的事，
裡面其實藏著一點好的東西。

你現在看不到沒關係，
時間會慢慢讓你看見。

先別急著下結論。
zh-tw.editorNote: 在剛經歷挫折、還在氣自己的時候看。不是叫你硬要正向，是提醒你現在的視角還太近，有些意義要拉開時間才看得清。
variations: 現在像爛事的，過幾年回頭看，常常是轉折點。
別在故事還沒寫完的時候，就先判它是悲劇。

en.content:
The things that look like failures right now
usually have a little something good buried inside.

It's okay that you can't see it yet.
Time will slowly let you.

Don't rush to the verdict.
en.editorNote: For the moment right after a setback, while you're still mad at yourself. Not a push to force positivity — just a reminder that you're standing too close to see the meaning, and some of it only comes into focus with distance.
variations: What feels like a disaster now is often, years later, the turning point.
Don't call it a tragedy while the story's still being written.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by consistency-over-motivation themes; rewritten in original wording

zh-tw.content:
你不用每天都很拚，
你只要每天都有出現。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合給那種一旦做不到 100 分就乾脆整個放棄的人。提醒他：穩定地出現，比偶爾爆發更難得，也更有用。
variations: 感覺對了才動，你會永遠在等；先動了，感覺才會跟上來。
撐住的不是熱血，是那個沒什麼感覺還是照做的你。

en.content:
You don't have to go all in every day.
You just have to keep showing up.
en.editorNote: For the all-or-nothing type who quits the whole thing the moment they can't do it perfectly. Showing up consistently beats the occasional burst.
variations: If you wait to feel like it, you'll be waiting forever. Move first; the feeling catches up.
What carries you isn't motivation — it's the version of you that does it anyway.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'run your own pace, don't compare' themes; fully rewritten

zh-tw.content:
別人跑得快不代表你慢，
你們根本不在同一條跑道上。
zh-tw.editorNote: 滑手機看到別人升職、結婚、買房而開始焦慮時看。提醒你每個人的時區不一樣，你沒有遲到。
variations: 你不是落後，你只是在走一條沒那麼多人走的路。
把眼睛從別人身上收回來，你會發現自己其實走得不錯。

en.content:
Someone running faster doesn't make you slow.
You're not even on the same track.
en.editorNote: For the moment you're scrolling and feel behind because everyone's getting promoted, married, buying homes. Everyone's on their own timeline — you're not late.
variations: You're not falling behind. You're just on a road fewer people take.
Take your eyes off everyone else and you'll notice you're actually doing fine.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'small efforts compound' themes; rewritten in original phrasing

zh-tw.content:
今天的一點點，
看起來什麼都不是，
但它正在偷偷幫你存。
zh-tw.editorNote: 進度看不出來、開始懷疑努力有沒有用的時候看。小事重複久了會變大事，只是利息要過一陣子才看得到。
variations: 每天一點點，加起來就是別人追不上的距離。
你現在埋的種子，不會明天就開花，但總有一天會。

en.content:
Today's little bit
looks like nothing,
but it's quietly saving up for you.
en.editorNote: For when progress feels invisible and you're doubting the effort. Small things repeated long enough become big things — the interest just takes a while to show.
variations: A little every day adds up to a lead nobody can catch.
The seeds you plant now won't bloom tomorrow, but someday they will.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'keep going, don't stop' themes; fully rewritten

zh-tw.content:
走多慢都沒關係，
只要你還沒把自己停下來。
zh-tw.editorNote: 累到只想躺平、覺得自己快撐不住的時候看。不是逼你加速，是告訴你慢著走也算數，停下來才是真的輸。
variations: 今天能做的，就是別放棄，這樣就夠了。
你不需要衝刺，你只需要不要往回走。

en.content:
It's okay however slow you go,
as long as you don't stop yourself.
en.editorNote: For when you're wiped out and feel like you can't keep going. Not a push to speed up — going slow still counts; stopping is the only real loss.
variations: All today asks of you is that you don't quit. That's enough.
You don't need to sprint. You just need to not walk backwards.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by self-encouragement themes; rewritten with original wording

zh-tw.content:
你已經比你以為的，
撐得更久、走得更遠了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 覺得自己一事無成、只想數還沒做到的事的時候看。提醒他回頭看看，那些咬牙撐過來的日子也是成績。
variations: 別只盯著還沒到的地方，看看你已經離出發點多遠了。
光是走到今天這裡，就已經很不簡單了。

en.content:
You've already held on longer
and come further than you give yourself credit for.
en.editorNote: For when you feel like you've accomplished nothing and keep counting what's undone. Look back — the days you gritted through are wins too.
variations: Stop staring at how far you have to go. Look at how far you've come.
Just making it to today is no small thing.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Mary Anne Radmacher resilience theme; rewritten with original wording

zh-tw.content:
勇敢不一定是大聲喊出來的，
有時候它只是你睡前小聲說：「明天再試一次。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那些覺得自己今天又搞砸、不夠勇敢的人。提醒他願意明天再來過，本身就是一種勇氣，不用轟轟烈烈。
variations: 撐不下去的時候還願意說「明天再來」，這就已經很勇敢了。
勇氣不是不怕，是怕著怕著還是動了。

en.content:
Courage isn't always loud.
Sometimes it's just you whispering at night, "I'll try again tomorrow."
en.editorNote: For anyone who feels they fell short or wasn't brave enough today. Being willing to come back tomorrow is its own kind of courage — it doesn't have to be dramatic.
variations: Still willing to say "tomorrow" when you're running on empty? That's already brave.
Courage isn't being unafraid. It's moving while you're still scared.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Julie Andrews persistence theme; rewritten with original wording

zh-tw.content:
所謂堅持，
就是失敗了十九次，第二十次還是去做了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給快要放棄的人。不是要他假裝沒受傷，而是提醒他：成功常常只差那一次沒被嚇退的嘗試。
variations: 你不是運氣差，你只是還沒走到那次會成的嘗試。
別人停在第十九次，你多走一步就贏了。

en.content:
Persistence is just failing nineteen times
and showing up for the twentieth anyway.
en.editorNote: For someone on the verge of quitting. Not about pretending it doesn't sting — just a reminder that success is often one un-scared attempt away.
variations: You're not unlucky. You just haven't reached the try that works yet.
Everyone else stopped at nineteen. One more step and it's yours.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by daily-effort accumulation theme; rewritten with original wording

zh-tw.content:
別小看每天一點點，
沙子一粒一粒堆，總有一天會變成一座山。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給覺得努力沒有回報、進度慢到看不見的人。提醒他成果是慢慢長出來的，現在看不到不代表沒在累積。
variations: 今天的一點點，是未來那座山的第一把沙。
進步常常安靜到你以為自己沒動，其實早就堆高了。

en.content:
Don't underestimate a little each day.
Grain by grain, the sand eventually becomes a mountain.
en.editorNote: For anyone who feels their effort isn't paying off or progress is too slow to see. The results grow quietly — invisible now doesn't mean nothing's adding up.
variations: Today's little bit is the first handful of sand in tomorrow's mountain.
Progress is often so quiet you think you've stalled — you've actually been stacking up the whole time.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Martin Luther King Jr. keep-moving-forward theme; rewritten with original wording

zh-tw.content:
飛不動就用跑的，
跑不動就用走的，
真的不行就用爬的——
反正方向別停在原地就好。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給狀態很差、只能慢慢來的人。重點不是速度，是別調頭、別停下，用什麼姿勢前進都算數。
variations: 今天能爬就爬，明天說不定就能走了。
再慢都是往前，只要你不掉頭。

en.content:
Can't fly? Run.
Can't run? Walk.
Can't walk? Crawl —
just keep facing forward.
en.editorNote: For someone who's drained and can only move slowly. It's not about speed; it's about not turning around. Any posture counts as long as you're still going.
variations: Crawl today if that's all you've got — you might walk tomorrow.
However slow, it's still forward, as long as you don't double back.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by failure-as-restart theme; rewritten with original wording

zh-tw.content:
跌倒不丟臉，
躺在地上不想起來才有點可惜。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給剛搞砸一件事、正在自我懷疑的人。失敗本身不是終點，重點是你願不願意拍拍灰再站起來。
variations: 每一次跌倒，都是離成功更近的一步——只要你站得起來。
失敗不會定義你，你站起來的次數才會。

en.content:
Falling isn't the embarrassing part.
Staying down because you don't feel like getting up — that's the waste.
en.editorNote: For someone who just messed up and is spiraling into self-doubt. Failure isn't the end; what matters is whether you dust off and stand back up.
variations: Every fall is a step closer to success — as long as you get up.
Failure doesn't define you. How many times you stand back up does.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'consistency beats motivation' theme; rewritten with original wording

zh-tw.content:
熱情會退燒，
但習慣會替你撐住場面。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種一開始很拚、後來動力消失就停擺的人。提醒他靠的不是天天熱血，而是把該做的事變成習慣。
variations: 等有動力才做，常常等不到；先做，動力才會來。
撐住你的不是那股衝勁，是每天乖乖重複的小動作。

en.content:
Motivation cools off.
Habits are what hold the line for you.
en.editorNote: For the person who starts strong then stalls the moment the drive fades. The reminder: you don't run on daily fire, you run on turning the work into a habit.
variations: If you wait to feel like it, you'll be waiting a while. Start, and the drive shows up.
What carries you isn't the burst of energy — it's the small thing you quietly repeat every day.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by self-belief 'halfway there' theme; rewritten with original wording

zh-tw.content:
你願意相信自己的那一刻，
難的事就已經做完一半了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給準備迎接挑戰、卻先被自我懷疑絆住的人。先相信自己做得到，剩下的一半交給行動慢慢補。
variations: 你怎麼看自己，世界大概也會跟著怎麼對你。
別等別人點頭，先給自己一個肯定。

en.content:
The moment you decide to believe in yourself,
the hard part is already half done.
en.editorNote: For someone facing a challenge but tripped up by self-doubt first. Believe you can, and let action fill in the other half.
variations: How you see yourself tends to shape how the world treats you.
Don't wait for someone else's nod — give yourself the green light first.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'bend without breaking' resilience theme; rewritten with original wording

zh-tw.content:
堅強不是假裝不痛，
是邊痛邊把日子過下去。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給正在硬撐、又被人說要堅強的人。承認自己受傷不代表軟弱，能帶著傷繼續走才是真正的韌性。
variations: 可以彎，但不要折斷；可以慢，但別假裝沒事。
哭完記得擦乾，然後繼續——這就夠勇敢了。

en.content:
Being strong isn't pretending it doesn't hurt.
It's getting through the day while it still does.
en.editorNote: For someone toughing it out who keeps being told to 'be strong.' Admitting you're hurt isn't weakness — carrying the wound and walking on is real resilience.
variations: Bend, but don't break. Go slow, but don't fake being fine.
Cry it out, wipe your face, keep going — that's brave enough.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'run your own race' theme; rewritten with original wording

zh-tw.content:
別老盯著別人跑多快，
你又不是在跑他的路。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給因為和別人比較而焦慮的人。每個人的賽道和節奏都不一樣，專注在自己有沒有往前，比贏過誰更重要。
variations: 和昨天的自己比就好，那才是你真正的對手。
慢沒關係，走錯方向才需要擔心。

en.content:
Stop watching how fast everyone else runs.
You're not even on their track.
en.editorNote: For anyone anxious from comparing themselves to others. Everyone's lane and pace differ — whether you're moving forward matters more than beating anyone.
variations: Just race yesterday's version of you — that's your real competition.
Slow is fine. Wrong direction is the only thing worth worrying about.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by the 'excellence is a habit, not an act' theme; rewritten with original wording

zh-tw.content:
厲害不是某一天突然發生的，
是你每天偷偷做的那些小事，加起來的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合貼在書桌前或健身計畫旁，提醒自己改變來自重複，而不是某次爆發的決心。
variations: 你重複做的事，最後會變成你這個人。
今天做一點點，明天就少後悔一點點。

en.content:
Greatness doesn't show up one big day.
It's the small stuff you quietly repeat, added up.
en.editorNote: Good for a desk note or a fitness plan reminder — change comes from repetition, not a single burst of willpower.
variations: What you repeat is what you become.
Do a little today, regret a little less tomorrow.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'doubt kills more dreams than failure' theme; rewritten with original wording

zh-tw.content:
讓你停下來的，從來不是失敗，
是那句「我大概做不到吧」。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給在還沒開始前就先否定自己的人。先去做，讓結果告訴你答案，而不是讓恐懼先說了算。
variations: 毀掉夢想的不是跌倒，是連試都不敢試。
你怕的事，做完才知道根本沒那麼可怕。

en.content:
What stops you is rarely failure.
It's that quiet "I probably can't."
en.editorNote: For anyone who shuts themselves down before they even start. Act first, let the result answer — don't let fear get the first word.
variations: Falling doesn't kill dreams. Never trying does.
Most things you fear feel small once they're actually done.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'life doesn't get easier, we get stronger' theme; rewritten with original wording

zh-tw.content:
日子不會變得比較好過，
是你慢慢變得扛得住了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合給正在硬撐的人。痛苦不一定會消失，但你的承受力會長大，回頭看會發現自己已經不一樣了。
variations: 難關沒有變小，是你長大了。
你以為撐不過去的，後來都成了你的底氣。

en.content:
Life doesn't get lighter.
You just slowly grow strong enough to carry it.
en.editorNote: For someone gritting their teeth right now. The weight may not vanish, but your capacity grows — look back and you'll see how far you've come.
variations: The hard part didn't shrink. You grew.
What you thought would break you became your backbone.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'never too old to set a new goal' theme; rewritten with original wording

zh-tw.content:
重新開始沒有截止日期，
你想開始的那天，就是最早的一天。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給覺得「太晚了」而遲遲不敢動的人。年齡不是門檻，今天永遠是剩下日子裡最年輕的一天。
variations: 種樹最好的時間是十年前，其次就是現在。
別問來不來得及，先問你想不想要。

en.content:
Starting over has no deadline.
The day you decide to begin is the earliest day there is.
en.editorNote: For anyone frozen by "it's too late." Age isn't a gate — today is the youngest you'll ever be again.
variations: The best time to plant a tree was years ago. The second best is now.
Don't ask if there's still time. Ask if you still want it.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'successful people feel doubt but don't let it stop them' theme; rewritten with original wording

zh-tw.content:
勇敢不是不害怕，
是手在抖，但你還是按下了開始鍵。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在重要決定或上台前給自己打氣。會緊張很正常，重點是別讓緊張替你做決定。
variations: 怕歸怕，腳還是往前走，那就叫勇敢。
等不怕了再做，那一天通常不會來。

en.content:
Brave isn't feeling no fear.
It's your hands shaking and still hitting start.
en.editorNote: A pep talk before a big decision or stepping on stage. Nerves are normal — just don't let them make the call for you.
variations: Scared, but moving forward anyway — that's the whole definition.
If you wait until you're not afraid, that day rarely comes.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'consistency over intensity' and small-efforts-add-up themes; rewritten with original wording

zh-tw.content:
你不用每天進步一大步，
每天進步一點點，一年後也認不出自己了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合提醒自己別追求一夜變強，貼在書桌或健身房置物櫃上，提醒「累積」才是真功夫。
variations: 今天的一點點，是明年的一大截。
別小看每天那一點，時間會幫你乘起來。

en.content:
You don't need one giant leap a day.
A little better every day, and in a year you won't recognize yourself.
en.editorNote: A reminder that you don't have to transform overnight. Good for a desk note or gym locker — the magic is in stacking small days.
variations: Today's little bit is next year's huge head start.
Don't underestimate the daily little — time multiplies it for you.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Churchill's 'failure to failure without losing enthusiasm' resilience theme; fully rewritten

zh-tw.content:
厲害的人不是沒跌倒過，
是跌倒之後，眼神還是亮的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在剛搞砸一件事、又得馬上振作的時候給自己看。失敗不可怕，可怕的是把熱情也一起摔丟了。
variations: 輸了一場，不代表整局都輸。
跌倒沒關係，別把站起來的力氣也一起丟了。

en.content:
Strong people aren't the ones who never fall.
They're the ones whose eyes still light up after.
en.editorNote: For the moment right after you've blown something and have to bounce back. Failing isn't the danger — dropping your spark along with it is.
variations: Losing one round doesn't mean you lost the whole game.
Falling is fine. Just don't throw away the energy to stand back up.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by '沒有不進步的人生，只有不進取的人' theme from Taiwan motivational quote collections; rewritten

zh-tw.content:
沒有走不通的路，
只有還沒願意轉彎的人。
zh-tw.editorNote: 卡關、覺得人生死路一條的時候看。路是死的，人是活的，換個方向不叫放棄，叫想通了。
variations: 此路不通，那就繞一條更漂亮的。
轉彎不是退步，是替自己找更好的路。

en.content:
There's no road that leads nowhere —
only people not yet willing to turn.
en.editorNote: For when you feel cornered and out of options. Roads are fixed; you're not. Changing direction isn't quitting — it's finally getting it.
variations: If this way's blocked, take a prettier detour.
Turning isn't going backward — it's finding yourself a better path.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'self-compassion during setbacks' and 'progress not perfection' themes; rewritten

zh-tw.content:
對自己兇一點不會比較快，
對自己溫柔一點才走得遠。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給那種一不順就猛罵自己的人。逼太緊容易斷，留點餘地反而撐得久。
variations: 你已經很努力了，這句也是真的。
休息不是偷懶，是為了走更長的路。

en.content:
Being hard on yourself won't make you faster.
Being kind to yourself makes you last.
en.editorNote: For the person who beats themselves up the second things go sideways. Push too hard and you snap; leave some slack and you go the distance.
variations: You've been trying hard. That's true too.
Rest isn't slacking — it's fuel for the long road.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'no one decides your future more than you' theme from success quote collections; rewritten

zh-tw.content:
別人可以看扁你，
但坐上駕駛座的，始終是你自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 被別人唱衰、不被看好的時候看。嘴巴長在別人身上，方向盤握在你手上，誰也搶不走。
variations: 別人怎麼說是他的事，怎麼走是你的事。
把你寫死的人很多，但結局還是你自己寫。

en.content:
People are free to count you out,
but you're the one in the driver's seat.
en.editorNote: For when others doubt you or write you off. Their mouths are theirs; the wheel is in your hands, and nobody can grab it.
variations: What they say is on them. Where you go is on you.
Plenty of people will write your ending — you still get the final draft.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Taiwan positive-energy quote themes about progress versus stagnation

zh-tw.content: 沒有不會進步的人生，只有不想前進的自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合貼在覺得自己卡關、原地踏步的時候看。重點不是你走多快，而是你還願不願意往前。
variations: 進步不會自己發生，但只要你願意動，它一定追得上你。
你不是沒有變好，只是還沒看見自己走了多遠。

en.content: There's no life that can't grow — only a self that doesn't want to move.
en.editorNote: Good for the days you feel stuck and spinning in place. It's not about how fast you go, but whether you still choose to go.
variations: Progress won't happen on its own, but the moment you move, it catches up.
You haven't stopped improving — you just haven't looked back to see how far you've come.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by persistence-themed success quotes about keeping going until results appear

zh-tw.content: 想要的結果還沒出現，不代表它不會來，只代表你還沒走到。
zh-tw.editorNote: 努力很久卻看不到成果的時候看這句。很多人是在快成功的那一步放棄的，別當那個人。
variations: 結果遲到了，不等於缺席，繼續走就對了。
你以為的白費力氣，其實都在路上排隊等你。

en.content: The result you want hasn't shown up yet — that doesn't mean it won't, just that you haven't arrived.
en.editorNote: For when you've grinded for ages with nothing to show. Most people quit one step before it works. Don't be that person.
variations: Late isn't the same as never. Keep walking.
All that effort you think was wasted is just standing in line, waiting for you.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by resilience quote themes about setbacks being part of the comeback

zh-tw.content: 每一次被打趴，都是下一次站更穩的練習。
zh-tw.editorNote: 搞砸了、跌倒了的時候看。挫折不是來打擊你的，是來幫你把下盤練紮實的。
variations: 倒下不可怕，可怕的是你以為自己站不起來。
現在的這一跤，是你以後笑著講出來的故事。

en.content: Every time you get knocked flat is practice for standing steadier next time.
en.editorNote: For the days you messed up or fell hard. Setbacks aren't here to break you — they're here to strengthen your footing.
variations: Falling isn't the scary part. Believing you can't get back up is.
This faceplant is just the story you'll tell with a grin later.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by self-belief themes about being halfway there once you believe

zh-tw.content: 你相信自己的那一刻，路就已經走了一半。
zh-tw.editorNote: 要做一件沒把握的事之前看。很多時候卡住你的不是難度，是那句「我大概不行」。
variations: 還沒開始就先說不行，那是替自己提前認輸。
信心不是等成功才有，是先有了它，成功才追得上。

en.content: The moment you believe in yourself, you're already halfway down the road.
en.editorNote: Read this before doing the thing you're unsure about. Usually it's not the difficulty stopping you — it's the quiet "I probably can't."
variations: Calling it quits before you start is just losing early.
Confidence doesn't wait for success. It comes first, and success catches up.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by quotes about success being small efforts repeated daily

zh-tw.content: 厲害的人不是一次做很多，是每天都做一點。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合提醒自己別追求一夜暴衝。真正拉開差距的，是那些別人看不見的、每天的一小步。
variations: 今天的一點點，是未來那個你拼起來的。
別小看每天的累積，時間最後會幫你算總帳。

en.content: Impressive people don't do a lot at once — they do a little every day.
en.editorNote: A reminder not to chase the overnight sprint. What really pulls you ahead is the small daily step nobody sees.
variations: Today's little bit is what tomorrow's you is built from.
Don't underestimate the daily stack — time settles the tab in your favor.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by quotes on fear killing more dreams than failure, and wanting things on the other side of fear

zh-tw.content: 殺死夢想的從來不是失敗，是那句「我怕我做不到」。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在害怕到不敢開始時看。失敗頂多讓你跌一次，但恐懼會讓你一輩子站在原地。
variations: 你想要的東西，幾乎都放在恐懼的另一邊。
怕，就帶著怕去做；等不怕的那天，機會早就走了。

en.content: What kills your dreams isn't failure — it's the line "I'm scared I can't."
en.editorNote: Read this when fear is keeping you from even starting. Failure trips you once; fear keeps you standing still forever.
variations: Almost everything you want is parked on the other side of fear.
Scared? Go do it scared. Wait to be fearless and the chance is already gone.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Carl Bard's quote about not making a brand new start but a brand new ending

zh-tw.content: 你回不到開頭重來，但你隨時可以從現在開始，寫一個不一樣的結局。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合覺得「太晚了、來不及了」的時候看。過去定不了你的全部，能改的永遠是接下來那一段。
variations: 重新開始不一定要回到原點，從這一秒轉彎也算。
前面寫壞了沒關係，後面的篇幅還空著，筆在你手上。

en.content: You can't go back and rewrite the start, but starting now you can write a whole different ending.
en.editorNote: For the moments you feel it's too late. Your past doesn't get the final say — the part you can still change is whatever comes next.
variations: Starting over doesn't mean going back to zero — turning the wheel right now counts too.
The opening got messy, fine. The rest of the page is blank, and you're holding the pen.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Carol Dweck's 'the power of yet' growth mindset concept

zh-tw.content: 你不是做不到，你只是「還沒」做到。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合學新東西卡關、開始懷疑自己的時候。差一個「還沒」，挫折就從句點變成逗點。
variations: 把「我不會」改成「我還不會」，後面就多了一條路。
現在卡住不代表永遠卡住，只代表你還在半路上。

en.content: It's not that you can't — you just can't yet.
en.editorNote: For when you're stuck learning something new and starting to doubt yourself. One word, "yet," turns a dead end into a comma.
variations: Swap "I can't do this" for "I can't do this yet" and a path opens up behind it.
Stuck now doesn't mean stuck forever — it just means you're still mid-road.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by resilience quotes about pain producing strength and setbacks setting up comebacks

zh-tw.content: 每一次跌倒，其實都是在替下一次站起來鋪路。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合剛經歷挫敗、還沒緩過來的時候看。痛沒辦法跳過，但痛過之後留下的力氣是真的。
variations: 現在這個低谷，是回頭看才看得懂的轉折點。
被打趴不丟臉，趴著不肯動才是真的輸。

en.content: Every fall is quietly laying the ground for the next time you stand up.
en.editorNote: For right after a setback, before you've bounced back. You can't skip the pain, but the strength it leaves behind is real.
variations: This low point is the plot twist you'll only understand looking back.
Getting knocked down isn't the shame — staying down by choice is.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Arthur Ashe's 'start where you are, use what you have, do what you can'

zh-tw.content: 從你現在的位置開始，用你手上有的東西，做你做得到的事。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合什麼都還沒準備好、卻一直想等「對的時機」的人。條件永遠不會剛好，先動再說。
variations: 別等萬事俱備，多數人就是等到最後什麼都沒做。
起點不用很完美，能動的那一步才是最值錢的。

en.content: Start where you are, use what you've got, do what you can.
en.editorNote: For anyone who feels unprepared and keeps waiting for the "right time." The conditions are never perfect — move first, adjust later.
variations: Don't wait for everything to line up; most people wait so long they never start.
Your starting line doesn't have to be perfect — the step you can actually take is the valuable one.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by resilience themes about getting back up after setbacks

zh-tw.content: 別用跌倒幾次來定義我，看我每次都還爬得起來就好。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合剛經歷失敗、有點懷疑自己的人。重點從來不是你倒了幾次，而是你還願意站起來幾次。
variations: 倒下不丟臉，躺著不動才是。
跌倒是動作，站起來才是選擇。

en.content: Don't count how many times I fell. Count how many times I got back up.
en.editorNote: For someone fresh off a setback and doubting themselves. It was never about how many times you go down — it's about how many times you choose to rise.
variations: Falling isn't the embarrassing part. Staying down is.
Falling is what happens. Getting up is what you decide.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by persistence quotes emphasizing steady effort over talent

zh-tw.content: 撐不下去的時候再撐一下，多數人就是在那一下之前放棄的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合卡在瓶頸、想直接擺爛的人。很多突破其實就差在「再一下」，分享給快放棄的朋友剛剛好。
variations: 你想放棄的那一刻，常常離成功只剩一步。
終點通常藏在你以為自己撐不住的那一步後面。

en.content: When you feel like quitting, hold on a little longer — that's the exact spot where most people give up.
en.editorNote: For anyone stuck at a wall and ready to throw in the towel. So many breakthroughs are just one more push away. Send it to a friend about to quit.
variations: The moment you want to quit is often one step from where you wanted to be.
The finish line usually hides right past the point you thought you couldn't go on.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Taiwan quotes about changing yourself rather than the world

zh-tw.content: 想改變世界的人很多，願意先改變自己的人很少。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合老是抱怨環境、卻遲遲不動手的人。世界很難一次推動，但你可以從調整自己這一格開始。
variations: 與其等世界變好，不如先讓自己變強。
改變別人很累，改變自己才划算。

en.content: Everyone wants to change the world. Almost nobody wants to change themselves first.
en.editorNote: For the person who keeps blaming their circumstances but never makes a move. You can't shove the whole world at once, but you can start with the one square that's you.
variations: Instead of waiting for the world to get better, get stronger first.
Changing other people is exhausting. Changing yourself actually pays off.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by quotes on how luck meets preparation and effort

zh-tw.content: 運氣，是機會剛好撞上了你的準備。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合覺得自己只是「運氣差」的人。機會其實人人都有，差別在你有沒有先把自己準備好。
variations: 別人說你運氣好，其實是你私下準備得夠久。
機會敲門的時候，希望你已經穿好鞋了。

en.content: Luck is just opportunity bumping into the work you already put in.
