en.editorNote: For anyone who thinks they're simply "unlucky." Opportunities come around for everyone — the difference is whether you got yourself ready first.
variations: People call it luck. They didn't see how long you prepared in private.
When opportunity knocks, I hope you've already got your shoes on.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by resilience-as-a-muscle metaphors about building strength through hardship

zh-tw.content: 韌性是練出來的，每一次「我以為我撐不住」都是在加重量。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合正在硬撐、覺得快被壓垮的人。現在的難不是在消耗你，是在把你練得更強。
variations: 你扛過的每一關，都偷偷把你變強了一點。
撐過去之後你會發現，當初的重量現在剛好變成肌肉。

en.content: Resilience is a muscle, and every "I don't think I can handle this" is just adding weight to the bar.
en.editorNote: For someone gritting their teeth and feeling close to crushed. What's hard right now isn't draining you — it's training you.
variations: Every round you survived quietly made you a little stronger.
Once you get through it, the weight that nearly broke you becomes the muscle you carry.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by consistency-beats-motivation themes about showing up daily

zh-tw.content: 動力會退潮，習慣不會。撐住你的從來不是熱血，是你每天還願意做。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合那種一開始很有衝勁、現在卻覺得提不起勁的人。提醒對方：不用每天都很想做，做就對了。
variations: 等有動力才動，你會等很久；先動起來，動力才會跟上。
厲害的人不是每天都很有狀態，只是狀態差的時候照樣做。

en.content: Motivation comes and goes. Habits don't. What carries you isn't the hype — it's still showing up when the hype is gone.
en.editorNote: For the friend who started strong and is now running on empty. Remind them they don't have to feel like it — they just have to do it.
variations: Wait until you feel motivated and you'll wait forever. Move first, the motivation catches up.
Consistent people aren't always in the zone. They just keep going on the off days too.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'comparing your film to someone else's trailer' theme on comparison

zh-tw.content: 你在拿自己的幕後花絮，去比別人的精華片段。難怪覺得自己差。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合滑社群滑到開始懷疑人生的人。別人只放他最好的那一面，你卻拿全部的自己去比。
variations: 你看到的是別人的成果，不是他熬過的那些夜。
別人的高光時刻，不是你的日常該對標的東西。

en.content: You're comparing your behind-the-scenes to everyone else's highlight reel. No wonder you feel behind.
en.editorNote: For anyone spiraling after scrolling social media. People only post their best take — you're judging your whole self against it.
variations: You're seeing their results, not the nights they grinded to get there.
Someone else's best moment was never meant to be your daily benchmark.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'walk at your own pace, your journey isn't the same' themes

zh-tw.content: 你又沒跟誰賽跑，幹嘛逼自己用別人的速度走自己的路。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合被「同齡人都已經…」這種焦慮綁架的人。每個人的賽道不一樣，根本沒有共同的終點線。
variations: 走慢一點不代表走錯路，你只是還在路上而已。
別人的時間表是別人的，你按自己的節奏抵達就好。

en.content: You're not racing anyone. So stop forcing yourself to walk your road at someone else's speed.
en.editorNote: For anyone trapped by the "people my age already..." spiral. Different lanes, different finish lines — there's no shared one.
variations: Going slower doesn't mean you took a wrong turn. You're just still on the way.
Their timeline is theirs. You only have to arrive on your own.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'consistency is direction not speed, steady steps add up' theme

zh-tw.content: 重點不是你走多快，是你有沒有一直朝同一個方向走。小步小步，回頭看會嚇到自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合覺得自己進步太慢、快要放棄的人。穩定的方向比爆發的速度更會帶你走遠。
variations: 每天一點點，看起來沒什麼，加起來不得了。
別小看那些不起眼的小步，它們正在偷偷把你帶很遠。

en.content: It's not about how fast you move — it's whether you keep aiming the same direction. Tiny steps, and one day you'll shock yourself looking back.
en.editorNote: For someone convinced they're improving too slowly and ready to quit. Steady direction takes you further than sudden bursts of speed.
variations: A little each day looks like nothing. Stacked up, it's everything.
Don't underestimate the small steps — they're quietly carrying you a long way.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by perseverance theme: the hard work you do after you're tired of the hard work

zh-tw.content: 真正拉開差距的，是你已經累到不行、還願意再多撐一下的那段。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合貼在快放棄的瞬間。多數人停在「累了」，能再走一步的人，後面就沒什麼對手了。
variations: 撐不下去的時候，剛好就是別人放棄的時候。
贏的不是最有天分的，是最後還站著的那個。

en.content: What actually sets you apart is the stretch you push through after you're already exhausted.
en.editorNote: Save this for the moment you want to quit. Most people stop at 'tired.' The one who takes one more step suddenly has no competition.
variations: The moment you can't go on is usually the moment everyone else taps out.
It's not the most gifted who win — it's the one still standing at the end.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Taiwan quote theme: every failure is a chance to start over

zh-tw.content: 失敗不是把你打回原點，是讓你帶著經驗重新開始。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給剛搞砸一件事、正在自責的人。同樣是從頭來，這次的你已經不是當初那個你了。
variations: 跌倒不可怕，可怕的是跌倒之後就懶得起來。
這次失敗教你的事，下次會變成你的底氣。

en.content: Failing doesn't send you back to zero — it lets you start over with everything you just learned.
en.editorNote: For someone beating themselves up after a mess-up. Yes, you're starting again, but you're not the same person who started the first time.
variations: Falling isn't the scary part. Being too defeated to get back up is.
What this failure taught you becomes next time's quiet confidence.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by accumulation theme: small grains of sand eventually form a mountain of gold

zh-tw.content: 你今天做的那件小到看不出意義的事，未來會幫你撐起一整片風景。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合覺得自己努力都白費的人。金沙一粒一粒看不出什麼，堆久了就是一座金山。
variations: 別問這一點點有沒有用，問的是你願不願意每天都做。
今天的微不足道，是未來的理所當然。

en.content: The thing you do today that feels too small to matter is quietly building the whole view you'll stand on later.
en.editorNote: For anyone convinced their effort is wasted. One grain of sand looks like nothing; pile it up long enough and it's a mountain of gold.
variations: Don't ask whether this little bit helps — ask whether you'll do it every day.
Today's 'barely anything' becomes tomorrow's 'of course.'

items.sourceNote: Inspired by the theme that perseverance is many short races run one after another, not one long marathon

zh-tw.content: 撐下去不是要你一口氣跑完全程，是把一段段短短的路，一段接一段走完而已。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合覺得目標太遠、快被壓垮的人。把「撐到最後」拆成「撐過這一段」，整件事就沒那麼嚇人了。
variations: 你不用今天就抵達終點，你只要今天別停下來。
別盯著還有多遠，盯著下一步就好。

en.content: Holding on doesn't mean sprinting the whole distance in one breath — it's just finishing one short stretch, then the next.
en.editorNote: For anyone staring at a goal so far away it feels crushing. Break 'make it to the end' into 'make it through this stretch' and the whole thing stops being terrifying.
variations: You don't have to reach the finish today — you just have to not stop today.
Stop measuring how far is left. Just look at the next step.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Mary Anne Radmacher's idea that courage is sometimes the quiet voice saying 'I'll try again tomorrow'

zh-tw.content: 勇敢不一定是大聲喊衝，有時候只是夜裡那句很小聲的「明天再試一次」。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給今天被打趴、覺得自己一點都不勇敢的人。願意明天再來一次，本身就是一種很安靜的勇氣。
variations: 不是不害怕才叫勇敢，是怕得要命還願意再站起來。
你今天撐住了，這就已經夠勇敢了。

en.content: Brave isn't always shouting 'let's go' — sometimes it's the tiny voice at night whispering 'I'll try again tomorrow.'
en.editorNote: For someone who got knocked flat today and feels anything but brave. Being willing to show up again tomorrow is its own quiet kind of courage.
variations: Brave isn't feeling no fear — it's being scared out of your mind and getting up anyway.
You made it through today. That's already brave enough.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by the river-cuts-rock-through-persistence-not-power theme

zh-tw.content: 水能把石頭磨穿，靠的不是力氣大，是它從來沒停過。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合覺得自己天賦不夠、贏不過別人的人。你不一定要最強，你只要比昨天的自己多撐一天。
variations: 不是最猛的會贏，是不肯停的會贏。
你以為的沒效果，只是還沒到時間而已。

en.content: Water carves through stone not because it's strong, but because it never stopped.
en.editorNote: For anyone convinced they're not talented enough to keep up. You don't have to be the strongest — you just have to outlast yesterday's version of you.
variations: It's not the fiercest who wins, it's the one who refuses to stop.
What feels like 'it's not working' is often just 'it's not time yet.'

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'I haven't failed, I just haven't been beaten by failure' theme

zh-tw.content: 你不是沒失敗過，你只是沒被失敗說服而已。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給剛被現實狠狠教訓、開始懷疑自己的人。失敗只是事情的結果，不是你這個人的結論。
variations: 輸掉一場，不代表你這個人輸了。
失敗能說你做錯了，沒資格說你不行。

en.content: It's not that you've never failed — you've just never let failure talk you out of it.
en.editorNote: For someone the world just schooled hard, now starting to doubt themselves. Failure is the outcome of a thing, not the verdict on you.
variations: Losing one round doesn't mean you, the person, lost.
Failure gets to say you did it wrong. It doesn't get to say you're not enough.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'the only limit to tomorrow is your doubts of today' theme (FDR)

zh-tw.content: 擋住你明天的，往往不是別人，是你今天先替自己說的那句「我大概不行」。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合還沒開始就先在腦中放棄的人。先把唱衰自己的那句話刪掉，路才看得見。
variations: 最先看不起你的人，最好別是你自己。
你連試都還沒試，怎麼就先認輸了？

en.content: What blocks your tomorrow usually isn't other people — it's the 'I probably can't' you handed yourself today.
en.editorNote: For anyone who quits in their own head before they even start. Delete the line where you talk yourself down, and suddenly there's a path.
variations: The first person to underestimate you really shouldn't be you.
You haven't even tried yet — why are you already calling it a loss?

items.sourceNote: Inspired by the Japanese proverb 'fall seven times, stand up eight'

zh-tw.content: 跌倒幾次不是重點，重點是你每次都還願意站起來。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合剛經歷挫折、覺得自己一直在跌倒的人。提醒對方：跌幾次沒人在數，站起來的次數才算數。
variations: 你不是失敗很多次，你是練習站起來很多次。
倒下不丟臉，躺著不動才可惜。

en.content: How many times you fall isn't the point. What counts is that you still get up every single time.
en.editorNote: For someone fresh off a setback who feels like they keep hitting the ground. Nobody's counting the falls — they're counting the times you stood back up.
variations: You haven't failed a hundred times — you've practiced getting up a hundred times.
Falling isn't the embarrassing part. Staying down is.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Mark Twain's 'courage is action in spite of fear'

zh-tw.content: 勇敢不是不害怕，是怕得要死還是去做了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給以為要等到不緊張才能行動的人。會抖很正常，抖著做完才是真的勇敢。
variations: 勇氣不會等你準備好心情，它只看你有沒有跨出那一步。
等你不怕的那天才動，可能一輩子都不會動。

en.content: Brave isn't feeling no fear — it's being scared half to death and doing it anyway.
en.editorNote: For the person waiting to feel calm before they act. The shaking is normal; doing it while you shake is the brave part.
variations: Courage doesn't wait for your nerves to settle — it just checks if you took the step.
If you wait until you're not afraid, you might be waiting forever.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Robert Louis Stevenson's 'judge each day by the seeds you plant'

zh-tw.content: 別老盯著今天收成多少，看看你今天種下了什麼。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合努力很久卻看不到成果、開始懷疑自己的人。種子埋在土裡的時候你看不到，但它一直在長。
variations: 有些努力不會馬上開花，但它從來沒有白費。
今天的你在打地基，地基不漂亮，但房子靠它撐著。

en.content: Stop measuring today by what you harvested. Look at what you planted.
en.editorNote: For someone grinding away with no visible results yet, starting to doubt themselves. You can't see the seed underground, but it's growing the whole time.
variations: Some effort doesn't bloom right away — but it's never wasted.
Today you're pouring the foundation. It's not pretty, but the whole house stands on it.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by C.S. Lewis's 'never too old to set another goal'

zh-tw.content: 現在開始會不會太晚？不會，太晚的是你一直問自己這句話。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給覺得自己「年紀大了、來不及了」的人。重新設一個目標、做一個夢，從來沒有過期這回事。
variations: 你不是太老了，你只是被「太老了」這三個字騙了。
重新開始不分年齡，只分你今天要不要。

en.content: Is it too late to start now? No. The only thing that's late is how long you've been asking.
en.editorNote: For anyone convinced they've aged out of their dream. Setting a new goal has no expiration date — the only clock running is the one you started by hesitating.
variations: You're not too old — you just got fooled by the words 'too old.'
Starting over has no age limit. It only asks whether today's the day.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Arianna Huffington's idea that failure is part of success, not its opposite

zh-tw.content: 失敗不是成功的相反，它是成功的其中一段。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給剛跌一跤、開始懷疑自己的人。失敗不是岔路，它本來就在通往成功的這條路上。
variations: 你不是失敗了，你只是還沒走到後面那一段。
每個成功的故事，中間都藏著一堆沒人看到的搞砸。

en.content: Failure isn't the opposite of success — it's one of the chapters inside it.
en.editorNote: For someone who just stumbled and started doubting themselves. Failure isn't a detour off the road to success; it was always part of the road.
variations: You didn't fail — you just haven't reached the later chapters yet.
Every success story is stuffed with messes nobody ever sees.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by the 'progress over perfection, a steady inch forward' theme

zh-tw.content: 今天比昨天好一點點，就已經贏過那個原地打轉的你了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給卡在完美主義、覺得不做到滿分就乾脆不做的人。一點點也是往前，別小看那一公分。
variations: 不用做到一百分，只要不是停在原地就好。
進步一公分，也是把昨天甩在後面了。

en.content: A little better than yesterday already beats the version of you that stayed put.
en.editorNote: For the perfectionist who figures it's all-or-nothing. An inch forward is still forward — don't write off the inch.
variations: You don't need a perfect score. You just need to not stand still.
One inch of progress still leaves yesterday behind.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Eleanor Roosevelt's idea that facing fear builds strength and confidence

zh-tw.content: 每一次你正眼看著害怕的東西，你就又多拿到一點勇氣。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在做之前就先怕到不行的時候。勇氣不是不怕，是怕著還願意往前一步。
variations: 你怕的事做完之後，下一件就沒那麼嚇人了。
勇氣是你怕得要死，還是去了。

en.content: Every time you look the scary thing straight in the eye, you walk away with a little more courage.
en.editorNote: For the moment you're terrified before even starting. Courage isn't the absence of fear — it's being scared and stepping forward anyway.
variations: Once you get through the thing you feared, the next one looks a lot smaller.
Courage is being scared to death and going anyway.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by resilience themes around falling and getting back up

zh-tw.content: 跌倒不算什麼，重點是你每次都有再站起來。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合搞砸一件事、心情很差的時候。沒有人是靠不跌倒走到終點的，是靠每次都願意爬起來。
variations: 倒下七次，那就站起來第八次。
厲害的不是從來沒輸過，是輸了還願意再來一次。

en.content: Falling isn't the story. The story is that you got back up every single time.
en.editorNote: For the day you messed something up and feel low. Nobody reaches the finish line by never tripping — they get there by always standing back up.
variations: Fall down seven times, stand up eight.
The impressive part isn't never losing — it's losing and showing up again.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by quotes about small daily accumulation building up over time

zh-tw.content: 別小看每天一點點，金沙堆久了也是一座山。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在進度看起來微不足道、懷疑自己有沒有在前進的時候。把每天的小努力存起來，回頭看會嚇一跳。
variations: 一天看不出差別，一年就差很多了。
你現在做的每一點，都在偷偷幫未來的你鋪路。

en.content: Don't underestimate a little bit each day — grains of sand pile into a mountain.
en.editorNote: For when your progress looks too small to matter. Save up those tiny daily efforts and the view back will surprise you.
variations: One day shows nothing; one year shows everything.
Every small thing you do now is quietly paving the road for future you.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by quotes about doing simple things well consistently

zh-tw.content: 把簡單的事天天做好，就一點都不簡單了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合覺得自己做的事很平凡、沒什麼了不起的時候。能堅持的平凡，本身就很不平凡。
variations: 把基本功做到極致，就是別人追不上的厲害。
厲害的人不是做了多難的事，是把該做的事一直做下去。

en.content: Doing the simple things well every single day stops being simple at all.
en.editorNote: For when your work feels ordinary and unremarkable. An ordinary thing you can sustain is, by itself, extraordinary.
variations: Master the basics and you become the thing nobody can catch.
Impressive people don't do the hardest thing once — they keep doing the right thing.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by the idea that working for your own dream or someone else's

zh-tw.content: 你不為自己的夢想拚，就只能幫別人的夢想打工。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在猶豫要不要為自己想做的事踏出一步的時候，提醒你時間花在哪、人生就長成什麼樣。
variations: 你今天偷的懶，都是在替別人的目標加班。
夢想不會等你有空，它只會等你開始。

en.content: If you won't grind for your own dream, you'll just be clocking in for someone else's.
en.editorNote: For when you're hesitating to take a step toward what you actually want. Where your hours go is where your life goes.
variations: Every bit you slack off today is overtime for somebody else's goal.
Your dream won't wait until you're free — it only waits until you start.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by the common 'sum of small daily efforts' / progress-over-perfection theme

zh-tw.content: 你每天看不出進步，是因為山是被一鏟一鏟堆出來的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合覺得努力很久卻沒成果、快要放棄的時候。提醒對方累積是看不見的，直到某天突然就看見了。
variations: 今天的一小步，會在某天變成回頭看才嚇一跳的距離。
進步不是大爆發，是每天偷偷往上墊一點點。

en.content: You can't see your progress day to day because the mountain gets built one shovel at a time.
en.editorNote: For when you've worked hard for ages with nothing to show and you're ready to quit. Growth is invisible until, one day, it isn't.
variations: Today's tiny step becomes the distance that shocks you when you look back.
Progress isn't a big bang — it's quietly stacking a little higher every day.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Maya Angelou's 'changed but not reduced' resilience idea, fully rewritten

zh-tw.content: 發生的事可以改變你，但沒資格把你變小。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合送給剛經歷低潮、覺得自己被打碎的人。受傷可以，但別讓那件事定義你能變成誰。
variations: 你被生活折過，但你不是被折斷，是學會了彎。
傷會留疤，但疤是你撐過來的證明，不是你變弱的標記。

en.content: What happens to you is allowed to change you. It is not allowed to make you smaller.
en.editorNote: For someone fresh out of a rough patch who feels broken by it. Getting hurt is fine — just don't let that thing decide who you get to become.
variations: Life bent you, but you didn't snap — you learned how to flex.
Scars stay, but a scar is proof you made it through, not a mark that you got weaker.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'compare yourself to your past self, not others' theme, rewritten

zh-tw.content: 別跟別人比快慢，跟昨天的自己比就好，那才是你真正的對手。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合被同儕壓力或社群動態搞到焦慮的時候。每個人的起跑線不一樣，唯一公平的比較是你跟你自己。
variations: 你不用贏過所有人，你只要比昨天多懂一點點。
滑別人的人生只會讓你跌倒，看自己的腳步才走得穩。

en.content: Don't race other people. Race yesterday's version of you — that's the only opponent that counts.
en.editorNote: For when peer pressure or the feed has you spiraling. Everyone starts from a different line; the only fair comparison is you against you.
variations: You don't have to beat everyone — just know a little more than you did yesterday.
Scrolling other people's lives trips you up; watching your own steps keeps you steady.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'showing up is enough / healing can be slow' gentle-motivation theme

zh-tw.content: 有些日子，光是好好起床、把自己餵飽，就已經很勇敢了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合送給狀態很差、連基本生活都覺得吃力的人。不是每天都要拚成就，活著撐住本身就是一種了不起。
variations: 撐過今天就好，明天的事明天再說。
你不必每天都發光，偶爾只是沒熄掉，也算數。

en.content: Some days, just getting out of bed and feeding yourself is already brave.
en.editorNote: For someone running on empty who's struggling with even the basics. Not every day has to be productive — surviving and holding on is its own kind of strong.
variations: Just make it through today. Tomorrow can be tomorrow's problem.
You don't have to shine every day. Some days, not going out counts too.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by hard-work / fear-of-starting motivation theme, rewritten

zh-tw.content: 會怕，代表你在乎；別讓怕，變成你不做的藉口。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在害怕失敗、遲遲不敢開始的時候。緊張是正常的，把它當成在乎的證據，然後照做就對了。
variations: 勇敢不是不怕，是怕得要死還是去做了。
你怕的那件事，做完之後通常會變成你最驕傲的事。

en.content: Being scared means you care. Just don't let the fear become your excuse not to do it.
en.editorNote: For when fear of failing is keeping you from starting. Nerves are normal — treat them as proof you care, then do the thing anyway.
variations: Brave isn't fearless — it's being terrified and going for it anyway.
The thing you're scared of is usually the thing you'll be proudest of once it's done.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Bruce Lee / Marie Forleo consistency theme (long-term consistency over short-term intensity), rewritten

zh-tw.content: 你偶爾的爆發改變不了什麼，真正把你帶到遠方的，是每天都做的那一點點。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合三分鐘熱度、常常想一次做到位的人。提醒自己重點不是某天衝很猛，而是明天還會繼續做。
variations: 與其偶爾拚命，不如每天剛好。
厲害的人不是某一天很猛，是每一天都還在。

en.content: Your occasional bursts won't get you far. What actually carries you is the little bit you do every single day.
en.editorNote: For the all-or-nothing crowd who burn out after one big push. The point isn't going hard once — it's still showing up tomorrow.
variations: Don't go all-in once. Go a little, every day.
Impressive people aren't intense one day — they just keep showing up.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by setback-as-training reframe theme from Taiwan motivation roundups, rewritten

zh-tw.content: 把挫折當失敗，它就真的是失敗；把它當練功，它就只是過程。
zh-tw.editorNote: 卡關、被打槍、計畫整個歪掉的時候很受用。同一件事，你怎麼定義它，決定它把你打倒還是把你練大。
variations: 這不是壞事發生在你身上，是難題正在幫你升級。
輸一場不叫失敗，停在那裡才叫。

en.content: Call a setback a failure and it becomes one. Call it training and it's just part of the process.
en.editorNote: Handy when you hit a wall, get rejected, or watch a plan fall apart. Same event — how you label it decides whether it flattens you or levels you up.
variations: It's not bad luck happening to you — it's a hard level leveling you up.
Losing a round isn't failure. Staying down is.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Confucius 'how slowly you go as long as you don't stop' persistence theme, rewritten

zh-tw.content: 走多慢都沒關係，只要你還在走，終點就會慢慢靠近。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給覺得自己進度落後、忍不住跟別人比的人。比速度沒意義，沒停下來才是真本事。
variations: 別人衝刺是別人的事，你只要不掉頭就好。
慢沒關係，停了才可惜。

en.content: It doesn't matter how slow you go. As long as you keep moving, the finish line keeps getting closer.
en.editorNote: For anyone feeling behind and stuck comparing themselves to others. Speed isn't the flex — not quitting is.
variations: Let other people sprint. Your only job is to not turn around.
Slow is fine. Stopping is the waste.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Mary Anne Radmacher 'courage is the little voice that says I'll try again tomorrow' theme, rewritten

zh-tw.content: 勇敢不一定很大聲，有時候它只是今天累爆了，還小聲跟自己說：明天再試一次。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合撐得很辛苦、覺得自己沒什麼了不起的人。願意明天再來，就已經很勇敢了。
variations: 不是每次勇敢都要轟轟烈烈，願意再來一次也算。
今天先休息，明天我們再戰，這也是一種堅強。

en.content: Courage isn't always loud. Sometimes it's just being wiped out today and quietly telling yourself: I'll try again tomorrow.
en.editorNote: For someone barely hanging on who feels like they're not doing enough. Being willing to come back tomorrow already counts as brave.
variations: Not every brave moment is dramatic. Being willing to try again counts too.
Rest today, fight tomorrow — that's a kind of strong too.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Walter Elliot 'perseverance is many short races one after another' theme, rewritten

zh-tw.content: 堅持不是一口氣跑完馬拉松，是一段一段短跑，跑完一段，再撐下一段。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合被「還有好遠」嚇到、想直接放棄的人。把大目標切小，你只要先撐過眼前這一段就好。
variations: 別想著還剩多遠，先把今天這一步走完。
撐不住一整年沒關係，先撐住這個禮拜。

en.content: Perseverance isn't one giant marathon. It's a bunch of short sprints — finish one, catch your breath, start the next.
en.editorNote: For anyone paralyzed by how far there is to go. Shrink the goal. You only have to survive the stretch right in front of you.
variations: Stop counting how far is left. Just finish today's step.
Can't hold on for a whole year? Just hold on for this week.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Theodore Roosevelt 'believe you can and you're halfway there' theme, rewritten

zh-tw.content: 你願意相信自己做得到的那一刻，事情就已經完成一半了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給準備做某件事、卻先被自己嚇退的人。最難的關卡常常不是事情本身，是你敢不敢開始相信自己。
variations: 最大的阻礙不在外面，是你心裡那句「我可能不行」。
別人還沒看好你之前，至少先換你自己看好你。

en.content: The moment you let yourself believe you can do it, half the job is already done.
en.editorNote: For someone about to attempt something who's scaring themselves out of it. The hardest part usually isn't the task — it's daring to bet on yourself.
variations: The biggest obstacle isn't out there. It's the little "I probably can't" in your head.
Before anyone else believes in you, at least let yourself be the first.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Eleanor Brown 'you cannot serve from an empty vessel' theme, rewritten

zh-tw.content: 你沒辦法用一個空杯子倒水給別人，先把自己裝滿，不是自私，是常識。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給那種對別人很好、卻一直忘記照顧自己的人。休息和顧好自己不是偷懶，是你還能繼續付出的前提。
variations: 先把自己照顧好，剩下的才有力氣管。
對自己好一點，不用先問過誰。

en.content: You can't pour from an empty cup. Filling yourself up first isn't selfish — it's just common sense.
en.editorNote: For the person who's endlessly kind to others but keeps forgetting themselves. Rest and self-care aren't slacking — they're what lets you keep showing up.
variations: Take care of yourself first. Everything else needs you to have something left.
Be kind to yourself. You don't need anyone's permission.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Japanese proverb 'fall seven times, stand up eight', rewritten

zh-tw.content: 跌倒七次沒關係，重點是你站起來的次數比跌倒多一次。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合最近一直碰壁、開始懷疑自己的人。沒有人是一路順著走到終點的，差別只在跌倒後有沒有再爬起來。
variations: 你不是失敗很多次，你是練習站起來練了很多次。
倒下不算結束，躺著不起來才算。

en.content: Fall seven times — fine. Just make sure you stand up one more time than you fall.
en.editorNote: For anyone hitting wall after wall lately and starting to doubt themselves. Nobody reaches the finish line without falling. The only difference is who gets back up.
variations: You haven't failed a bunch of times — you've practiced getting up a bunch of times.
Falling isn't the end. Staying down is.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'you are enough' self-worth theme, fully rewritten

zh-tw.content: 你不用先變成別人眼中的樣子，才配得上好的事，你現在這個樣子，就已經夠了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給那種老是覺得自己還不夠好、要再瘦一點、再厲害一點才敢喜歡自己的人。你的價值不是拿來被審核的。
variations: 你已經很好了，只是你太習慣先看自己哪裡不夠。
不用等變得更好才愛自己，你就從現在開始。

en.content: You don't have to become someone else's idea of enough before you deserve good things. The version of you standing here already qualifies.
en.editorNote: For the person who always feels a little short — a bit thinner, a bit more impressive — before they'll allow themselves to feel worthy. Your value was never up for review.
variations: You're already enough. You're just too used to scanning for what's missing.
Don't wait to be better to love yourself. Start from right now.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'motivation is temporary, consistency is permanent' theme, rewritten

zh-tw.content: 動力是一時的，等它出現你就先睡著了；真正帶你走的，是就算沒動力也照做的那個習慣。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合那種一直在等「有動力」才要開始的人。動力靠不住，真正撐起你的是不靠心情也能執行的日常節奏。
variations: 別等有心情才做，養成不用心情也做得下去的習慣。
動力負責點火，紀律負責讓火不熄。

en.content: Motivation is a mood — wait for it and you'll fall asleep first. What actually carries you is the habit you keep even when the mood never shows up.
en.editorNote: For anyone stuck waiting to 'feel motivated' before they start. Motivation is unreliable. What holds you up is a routine that runs whether or not you're in the mood.
variations: Don't wait to feel like it. Build the habit that runs without the feeling.
Motivation lights the fire. Discipline keeps it from going out.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Einstein 'strive to be of value' theme, rewritten

zh-tw.content: 別急著證明自己有多成功，先想想你能為誰帶來一點價值，成功通常會自己跟上來。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給拼命想被看見、被肯定的人。與其追著「成功」跑，不如把力氣放在把事情做得對別人有用，回報往往是後來才發生的事。
variations: 把「我想贏」換成「我能幫上什麼」，路會突然變寬。
先做出價值，掌聲是附贈的。

en.content: Stop rushing to prove how successful you are. Ask who you can actually be useful to first — success tends to catch up on its own.
en.editorNote: For the person straining to be seen and validated. Instead of chasing 'success,' put your energy into being genuinely useful. The payoff usually shows up later.
variations: Swap 'I want to win' for 'what can I help with' and the road gets wider.
Build value first. The applause is a bonus.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Denzel Washington 'without consistency you'll never finish' theme, rewritten

zh-tw.content: 沒有那股衝勁你根本不會開始，但沒有天天做下去的耐性，你也永遠走不到終點。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合開頭很熱血、後面就慢慢放掉的人。開始靠一時的勇氣，完成靠的是把它變成每天都在做的事。
variations: 會開始的人很多，走到最後的人才少。
熱情負責起跑，堅持負責衝線。

en.content: Without the spark you'd never begin — but without the patience to keep showing up, you'll never reach the end either.
en.editorNote: For the person who starts on fire and quietly fades halfway. Starting takes a burst of courage; finishing takes turning it into something you do every day.
variations: Plenty of people start. Far fewer make it to the end.
Passion handles the starting line. Persistence handles the finish.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'comparison is the thief of joy' theme, rewritten in own words

zh-tw.content: 你滑手機看到的都是別人的精華片段，卻拿來跟自己的幕後花絮比，難怪怎麼比都覺得自己輸。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給老是覺得別人過得比較好的人。別人放上來的是剪過的成果，你看到的自己卻是連 NG 都算進去的完整版，這種比法本來就不公平。
variations: 把注意力從「別人跑多快」拉回「我今天有沒有比昨天前進一點」。
羨慕別人的花園前，先記得你只看得到他們的圍牆外面。

en.content: Everything you scroll past is someone else's highlight reel, and you're comparing it to your own behind-the-scenes footage — no wonder you always feel like you're losing.
en.editorNote: For anyone who keeps feeling like everyone else has it better. What people post is the edited final cut; what you see of yourself includes every blooper. That match was never fair to begin with.
variations: Move your focus from 'how fast they're running' to 'did I get a little further than yesterday.'
Before you envy someone's garden, remember you're only seeing the outside of their fence.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'fail forward / failure is feedback' theme, rewritten

zh-tw.content: 失敗又不會扣你人生的分數，它只是很直接地告訴你：這條路走不通，換一條試試看。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合把失敗當世界末日的人。搞砸一次不是判你出局，而是這件事給你的回饋，聽懂它、調整方向，比什麼都值錢。
variations: 不是你不行，是那個方法不行，這兩件事差很多。
把「我又失敗了」翻譯成「我又多知道一個行不通的做法」。

en.content: Failure doesn't dock points from your life — it's just bluntly telling you this road's a dead end, so go try another one.
en.editorNote: For anyone who treats failure like the end of the world. Messing up once doesn't call you out of the game; it's the feedback the situation gives you. Hearing it and adjusting is worth more than anything.
variations: It's not that you can't do it — that approach couldn't. Those are very different things.
Translate 'I failed again' into 'I now know one more way that doesn't work.'

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'you don't have to be great to start' theme, rewritten

zh-tw.content: 你不用等到準備好才開始，因為你會發現，人是先開始了，才慢慢準備好的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給一直在「等時機」的拖延者。準備好是動起來以後才會發生的狀態，不是動之前的門票，先踏出去，能力會邊走邊長出來。
variations: 完美的起跑時機不存在，存在的只有「現在」跟「更晚」。
先做爛的第一版，總比放在腦裡發霉的完美版有用。

en.content: You don't have to wait until you're ready to start, because you'll find that people get ready by starting, not the other way around.
en.editorNote: For the procrastinator forever 'waiting for the right time.' Being ready is a state that shows up after you move, not a ticket you need before it. Step out first — the skills grow along the way.
variations: The perfect moment to start doesn't exist. Only 'now' and 'later' do.
A bad first draft beats a perfect one rotting in your head.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'you owe yourself rest / self-compassion' theme, rewritten

zh-tw.content: 你對別人溫柔到不行，卻總是對自己特別嚴苛，其實你也值得被你自己好好對待一次。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合永遠把自己排在最後一位的人。你會體諒朋友的疲憊，卻只會罵自己不夠努力。試著把那份溫柔留一點給自己，你也是需要被照顧的那個人。
variations: 你對朋友說的那些安慰的話，自己聽也一樣有用。
累了就休息，這不是偷懶，是讓自己能走更遠。

en.content: You're endlessly gentle with everyone else but brutally hard on yourself — you actually deserve to be treated well by you too, at least once.
en.editorNote: For the person who always puts themselves last. You'll understand a friend's exhaustion but only scold yourself for not trying harder. Try saving a little of that kindness for yourself. You're someone who needs looking after too.
variations: The comforting words you'd say to a friend work just as well on you.
Rest when you're tired — that's not slacking, it's how you go the distance.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'excuses kill dreams / fear kills more than failure' theme, fully rewritten

zh-tw.content: 殺死夢想的很少是失敗，多半是那句「等我準備好再說」。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合傳給那個計畫喊了三年還沒開始的朋友。真正拖垮我們的通常不是能力不夠，而是替自己找的那一堆藉口，先動起來，答案會在路上出現。
variations: 把「以後再說」翻譯成白話，就是「大概不會做」。
你怕的其實不是失敗，是被別人看見你在努力。

en.content: Dreams rarely die from failure — they mostly die from "I'll start once I'm ready."
en.editorNote: Send this to the friend who's been talking about the same plan for three years. What sinks us usually isn't a lack of ability, it's the pile of excuses we build. Move first, and the answers show up on the way.
variations: "Someday" is just a polite way of saying "probably never."
You're not really scared of failing — you're scared of being seen trying.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by anti-hustle 'you can be a work in progress and still be enough' theme, rewritten

zh-tw.content: 你可以還沒變成理想的自己，同時也已經夠好了，這兩件事並不衝突。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給一邊努力變好、一邊又嫌自己不夠的人。成長不代表現在的你有問題，你可以邊修邊愛自己，不用等到完成版才准許自己開心。
variations: 半成品也是有價值的，你不用蓋好整棟房子才配住進去。
進步跟接納自己，本來就可以同時發生。

en.content: You can still be becoming who you want to be and already be enough — those two things don't cancel each other out.
en.editorNote: For anyone who works on themselves while constantly feeling they fall short. Growing doesn't mean the current you is broken. You're allowed to love yourself mid-renovation, not just at the finished version.
variations: A work in progress still has worth — you don't have to finish the house to deserve living in it.
Getting better and accepting yourself were always allowed to happen at the same time.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Rocky 'how hard you can get hit and keep moving' theme, reworded to fresh imagery

zh-tw.content: 厲害的人不是沒被打倒過，是被打倒後還願意站起來拍拍灰塵再走。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合剛經歷挫折、覺得自己爛透了的時候。人生比的從來不是誰沒跌倒，而是誰跌倒後還能再爬起來，你現在的低潮不是結局，是中場休息。
variations: 跌倒不丟臉，賴在地上不想起來才可惜。
你能承受幾次重來，就決定了你能走多遠。

en.content: Strong people aren't the ones who never got knocked down — they're the ones who got up, dusted off, and kept walking anyway.
en.editorNote: For the moment right after a setback, when you feel like a total mess. Life was never about who never fell, but about who can get back up. This low point isn't the ending — it's halftime.
variations: Falling isn't the embarrassing part — staying down because you can't be bothered is.
How many restarts you can take is what decides how far you go.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'discipline is choosing what you want most over what you want now' theme, rewritten

zh-tw.content: 自律不是逼自己做討厭的事，是為了更想要的東西，暫時放下現在最想要的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合貼在書桌前或設成手機桌布。每次想偷懶時提醒自己，你不是在犧牲快樂，是在把快樂存起來，留給那個未來會感謝你的自己。
variations: 每個現在的小忍耐，都是在替未來的你鋪路。
想要的東西越大，願意延後的享受就要越多。

en.content: Discipline isn't forcing yourself to do things you hate — it's setting down what you want now for what you want most.
en.editorNote: Good as a sticky note on your desk or a phone wallpaper. Every time you want to slack off, remind yourself you're not sacrificing joy — you're saving it for the future you who'll thank you.
variations: Every small 'not right now' is you paving a road for the future you.
The bigger the thing you want, the more instant stuff you have to postpone.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'you can do hard things / comparison steals joy' theme, reworded

zh-tw.content: 別拿自己的幕後，去比別人精修過的幕前，你只是還沒走到讓人看見的那一段。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合每次滑完社群就自我懷疑的人。你看到的都是別人挑過、修過的高光，卻拿來對照自己最狼狽的日常，這場比賽從一開始就不公平，放過自己吧。
variations: 別人光鮮的十秒，背後可能是你沒看到的十年。
跟昨天的自己比就好，那才是你真正的對手。

en.content: Stop comparing your behind-the-scenes to someone else's edited highlight reel — you just haven't reached the part worth showing yet.
en.editorNote: For anyone who spirals into self-doubt after scrolling social media. What you see is other people's curated, filtered best, held up against your messiest day. That contest was rigged from the start — let yourself off the hook.
variations: Someone's shiny ten seconds might be built on ten years you never saw.
Just compare yourself to yesterday's you — that's your real opponent.

items.sourceNote: internal only

zh-tw.content: 你不需要一次跨出一大步，只要每天願意往前踏一小步，一年後回頭看，那已經是一段很長的路了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合新習慣養成、健身或學習進度停滯時的自我打氣。
variations: 別急著追求飛躍，穩穩地每天前進一點點，時間會替你把距離拉開。
小小的進步不起眼，但累積三百六十五天，就是別人追不上的差距。

en.content: You don't have to take one giant leap. Just be willing to take one small step forward each day, and a year from now you'll look back on a road that's surprisingly long.
en.editorNote: Good for building new habits, or fitness and study plateaus.
variations: Forget the big leap. Move a little every day, and time will stretch the gap for you.
Tiny progress feels like nothing, but stack up 365 days of it and it becomes a lead no one can catch.

items.sourceNote: internal only

zh-tw.content: 失敗不是終點，它只是把你不需要的路先幫你劃掉，讓你更快找到真正屬於你的那一條。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合創業受挫、面試落選或計畫失敗後的安慰語。
variations: 每一次失敗，都是在替你篩掉錯的選項，離對的答案又近了一步。
跌倒不可怕，可怕的是把跌倒當成句點，而不是逗號。

en.content: Failure isn't the end. It just crosses out the paths you didn't need, so you can find the one that's actually yours a whole lot faster.
en.editorNote: For comfort after a failed startup, rejected interview, or busted plan.
variations: Every failure filters out a wrong option and nudges you one step closer to the right answer.
Falling isn't the scary part. Treating the fall as a period instead of a comma is.

items.sourceNote: internal only

zh-tw.content: 別把自己的價值交給別人的一句評價來決定，你不是為了讓每個人都喜歡才活著的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合面對他人批評、社群比較或自我懷疑時使用。
variations: 一句否定不能定義你，除非你自己先簽名同意。
你不欠世界一個完美的樣子，你只欠自己一個真實的活法。

en.content: Don't hand over your worth to someone else's one-line opinion. You're not here to be liked by everyone.
en.editorNote: For facing criticism, social comparison, or self-doubt.
variations: One put-down can't define you, unless you sign off on it first.
You don't owe the world a perfect version of you. You just owe yourself an honest one.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by the theme that consistency, not intensity, is what compounds over time

zh-tw.content: 改變你人生的不是你偶爾拚一次的那一天，而是你每天都有做的那件小事。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合分享給正在建立習慣、覺得進度太慢想放棄的人，例如健身、學習或戒掉壞習慣。
variations: 厲害的不是三分鐘熱度那三分鐘，而是熱度過了還在做的那些日子。
你不用每天都拚盡全力，你只需要每天都別停下來。

en.content: It's not the one day you go all in that changes your life. It's the small thing you do every single day.
en.editorNote: Great for anyone building a habit and feeling like progress is too slow to matter — gym, studying, quitting something.
variations: The impressive part isn't the burst of motivation. It's still showing up after it fades.
You don't have to give it everything every day. You just have to not stop.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by the idea of comparing your behind-the-scenes to someone's highlight reel

zh-tw.content: 你拿自己的幕後花絮，去比別人剪好的精華片段，當然會覺得自己輸。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合滑社群滑到心情低落、覺得別人都過得比自己好的時候。
variations: 別人的限動只放了三秒的高光，你卻拿它來否定自己一整年。
你唯一該比的對象，是去年那個還沒開始的自己。

en.content: You're comparing your behind-the-scenes to someone else's highlight reel. Of course you feel like you're losing.
en.editorNote: For those moments after scrolling social media leaves you feeling like everyone's doing better than you.
variations: Their three-second highlight is not your whole year. Stop scoring yourself against it.
The only fair comparison is you versus who you were last year.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by the 'courage is the quiet voice that says I'll try again tomorrow' theme

zh-tw.content: 勇敢不一定是轟轟烈烈，有時候只是今天累到不行，還願意說一句「明天再試一次」。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合安慰那種撐得很辛苦、卻還沒放棄的朋友，也適合對自己說。
variations: 沒有大聲宣布的堅持，才是真正撐住你的那種。
撐不住的時候還在撐，這件事本身就已經很了不起了。

en.content: Courage isn't always loud. Sometimes it's just being exhausted and still saying, "I'll try again tomorrow."
en.editorNote: For comforting a friend who's barely holding on but hasn't quit — and for saying to yourself.
variations: The quiet kind of persistence, the kind nobody claps for, is the kind that actually carries you.
Still holding on when you feel like you can't is already something to be proud of.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by the 'success is small efforts repeated daily' compounding theme

zh-tw.content: 你不需要一次跨很大步，你只需要別停在原地等一個完美的時機。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合一直在計畫卻遲遲不動手、或等「準備好」才要開始的人。
variations: 小到不好意思說出口的進度，累積起來也能把你帶到很遠的地方。
完美時機不會自己來敲門，它是被你一步一步走出來的。

en.content: You don't need one giant leap. You just need to stop waiting in place for the perfect moment.
en.editorNote: For the chronic planner who keeps waiting to feel "ready" before actually starting.
variations: Progress so small you're embarrassed to mention it still adds up to somewhere far away.
The perfect moment doesn't knock. You build it one small step at a time.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by the 'greatest glory is rising every time you fall' resilience theme

zh-tw.content: 厲害的不是從來沒跌倒，而是每次跌倒都爬得起來。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合安慰剛經歷挫折、覺得自己很失敗的人，也適合放在自己看得到的地方當提醒。
variations: 跌幾次不算什麼，重點是你到現在還願意站起來。
沒有人是一路順順的，能撐到現在的你已經很強了。

en.content: The impressive part isn't never falling. It's getting back up every single time.
en.editorNote: For comforting someone fresh off a setback who feels like a failure — and for reminding yourself.
variations: Falling a few times doesn't define you. Still choosing to stand up does.
Nobody had a smooth ride here. The fact that you're still going means you're stronger than you think.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Steve Maraboli's 'life doesn't get easier, we get stronger' theme

zh-tw.content: 日子不會突然變輕鬆，是你慢慢變得扛得住。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合正在硬撐、覺得壓力沒有變小的人，提醒他其實是自己在長大，不是問題變簡單。
variations: 難的事沒有變少，是你變得更有辦法。
你會發現，同一種壓力，去年壓垮你，今年你只是嘆口氣就繼續走。

en.content: Life doesn't suddenly get lighter. You just slowly get strong enough to carry it.
en.editorNote: For someone gritting their teeth through a hard stretch who feels like nothing's easing up — remind them they're the one growing.
variations: The hard stuff didn't shrink. You got more capable.
The same weight that crushed you last year? This year you just sigh and keep walking.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by the 'be tender to yourself and the world softens' self-compassion theme

zh-tw.content: 對自己溫柔一點，世界的稜角也會跟著變鈍。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合送給對自己太嚴苛、動不動就自責的朋友，也適合貼在鏡子上提醒自己。
variations: 你怎麼對自己，就決定了這個世界怎麼對你。
先學會不罵自己，才有力氣去面對外面的難。

en.content: Be a little gentler with yourself, and the world's sharp edges start to dull too.
en.editorNote: For a friend who's brutally hard on themselves and self-blames over everything — or to stick on your own mirror.
variations: How you treat yourself quietly sets the tone for how the world treats you.
Learn to stop scolding yourself first. Then you'll have the strength for everything else.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by the 'self-worth starts when you stop explaining yourself' and 'you don't need permission to take up space' themes

zh-tw.content: 當你不再急著跟每個人解釋自己，你的價值就開始站穩了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合總是想討好、怕被誤會而不停解釋的人，提醒他不必事事求得別人認可。
variations: 你不需要別人點頭，才有資格佔一個位置。
懂你的人不用你多說，不懂的人你說再多也沒用。

en.content: The moment you stop rushing to explain yourself to everyone, your worth starts standing on its own.
en.editorNote: For the chronic people-pleaser who over-explains out of fear of being misunderstood — you don't need everyone's approval.
variations: You don't need anyone's nod to have the right to take up space.
The people who get you don't need the explanation. The ones who don't won't get it anyway.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Mary Anne Radmacher's 'courage is the quiet voice saying I'll try again tomorrow' theme

zh-tw.content: 勇敢不一定是大聲的，有時候只是睡前小聲跟自己說：明天再試一次。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合累到懷疑人生、只想撐過今天的人，提醒他願意明天再來一次，本身就是一種勇敢。
variations: 撐不下去的時候，願意明天再站起來就已經很了不起了。
真正的堅強常常沒有配樂，就只是默默把今天過完而已。

en.content: Courage isn't always loud. Sometimes it's just whispering to yourself before bed: I'll try again tomorrow.
en.editorNote: For someone worn down to the bone who just wants to survive today — being willing to come back tomorrow is its own kind of brave.
variations: When you can't push any further, being willing to get back up tomorrow is already huge.
Real strength usually has no soundtrack. It's just quietly getting through the day.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Brené Brown's 'sometimes the bravest thing is just showing up' theme

zh-tw.content: 有時候最勇敢的事，不是做得多好，而是你有出現。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合狀態很差、卻還是硬著頭皮去上班上課的人，提醒他光是有現身就贏了一半。
variations: 你不用表現得很厲害，你有到場就已經夠了。
很多時候，走進那扇門就是當天最難的一關。

en.content: Sometimes the bravest thing isn't doing it well — it's just showing up at all.
en.editorNote: For anyone who dragged themselves to work or class on empty — showing up when you're running on fumes already counts as a win.
variations: You don't have to be impressive. You just have to be there.
Half the time, walking through the door is the hardest part of the whole day.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Carrie Fisher's 'do it afraid, confidence follows the action' theme

zh-tw.content: 你不用先有信心才去做，是做了以後，信心才慢慢長出來。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合一直在等自己準備好、等自己有底氣的人，提醒他信心是行動的結果，不是前提。
variations: 帶著怕，還是去做，這才叫勇敢。
沒把握也沒關係，做過幾次，把握自己會來。

en.content: You don't need confidence before you act. You act, and then the confidence slowly grows in.
en.editorNote: For the person waiting to feel ready or bold enough — confidence is a result of doing, not a prerequisite for it.
variations: Scared and doing it anyway — that's the whole definition of brave.
It's fine if you're not sure. Do it a few times and the certainty shows up on its own.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Arnold Schwarzenegger's 'strength comes from the struggles you don't surrender to' theme

zh-tw.content: 讓你變強的，從來不是贏的時候，而是那些你很想放棄卻沒有放棄的時候。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合正卡在低潮、覺得自己很沒用的人，提醒他撐著沒倒的每一天都在累積力量。
variations: 你以為的白費力氣，其實都在偷偷把你練壯。
沒有一場硬仗是白打的，就算輸了，你也比昨天更耐打。

en.content: What makes you stronger was never the winning — it's all the times you wanted to quit and didn't.
en.editorNote: For someone stuck in a slump feeling useless — every day they hold on without collapsing is quietly building their strength.
variations: The effort you think was wasted is secretly making you tougher.
No hard fight is for nothing. Even the ones you lose leave you harder to knock down.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Abraham Maslow's 'growth must be chosen again and again' theme

zh-tw.content: 成長不是一次就決定好的事，是你每一天都要重新選一次要往前。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合覺得自己老是原地打轉、進三步退兩步的人，提醒他退回去很正常，願意再選前進才是重點。
variations: 沒有人是一次就長大的，都是選了無數次要繼續。
退回舒適圈很容易，難的是隔天又願意踏出去一次。

en.content: Growth isn't a one-time decision. It's choosing to move forward again, every single day.
en.editorNote: For anyone who feels like they keep circling back — falling back is normal; what matters is being willing to choose forward again.
variations: Nobody grows up in one go. It's choosing to keep going, a thousand times over.
Retreating to your comfort zone is easy. The hard part is stepping out again the next morning.
